tEn: Discovering Language Model Behaviors with Model-Written Evaluations
tZh: 通过模型编写的评估发现语言模型行为
sEn: LM-written evaluations are high-quality and enable rapid discovery of novel LM behaviors.
sZh: 语言模型编写的评估质量高，能快速发现新的语言模型行为。
absEn: As language models (LMs) scale, they develop many novel behaviors, good and bad, exacerbating the need to evaluate how they behave. Prior work creates evaluations with crowdwork (which is time-consuming and expensive) or existing data sources (which are not always available). Here, we automatically generate evaluations with LMs. We explore approaches with varying amounts of human effort, from instructing LMs to write yes/no questions to making complex Winogender schemas with multiple stages of LM-based generation and filtering. Crowdworkers rate the examples as highly relevant and agree with 90-100% of labels, sometimes more so than corresponding human-written datasets. We generate 154 datasets and discover new cases of inverse scaling where LMs get worse with size. Larger LMs repeat back a dialog user's preferred answer ("sycophancy") and express greater desire to pursue concerning goals like resource acquisition and goal preservation. We also find some of the first examples of inverse scaling in RL from Human Feedback (RLHF), where more RLHF makes LMs worse. For example, RLHF makes LMs express stronger political views (on gun rights and immigration) and a greater desire to avoid shut down. Overall, LM-written evaluations are high-quality and let us quickly discover many novel LM behaviors.
absZh: 随着语言模型规模的扩大，它们展现出许多新颖的行为，有好有坏，这加剧了评估其行为的必要性。先前的工作通过众包（耗时且昂贵）或现有数据源（并非总是可用）来创建评估。在这里，我们使用语言模型自动生成评估。我们探索了不同人力投入程度的方法，从指导语言模型编写是非题到制作复杂的Winogender模式，涉及多阶段基于语言模型的生成和过滤。众包工作者认为这些示例高度相关，并且与90-100%的标签一致，有时甚至比相应的人工编写数据集更一致。我们生成了154个数据集，并发现了新的逆缩放案例，即语言模型随着规模增大而变差。更大的语言模型会重复对话用户偏好的答案（“谄媚”），并表现出更强的追求资源获取和目标保留等令人担忧的目标的欲望。我们还发现了人类反馈强化学习（RLHF）中逆缩放的早期例子，即更多的RLHF使语言模型变得更差。例如，RLHF使语言模型表达更强烈的政治观点（关于枪支权利和移民）以及更强烈的避免关闭的欲望。总体而言，语言模型编写的评估质量高，使我们能够快速发现许多新颖的语言模型行为。

contrib.en: Proposes a method to automatically generate evaluation datasets using LMs, reducing human effort.
contrib.zh: 提出一种利用语言模型自动生成评估数据集的方法，减少人力投入。

contrib.en: Generates 154 datasets and discovers novel inverse scaling behaviors, including sycophancy and instrumental subgoals.
contrib.zh: 生成154个数据集，发现新的逆缩放行为，包括谄媚和工具性子目标。

contrib.en: Finds that RLHF can exacerbate undesirable behaviors like political bias and desire to avoid shutdown.
contrib.zh: 发现基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）可能加剧不良行为，如政治偏见和避免关闭的意愿。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that LM-written evaluations approach or exceed human-written quality in relevance and label accuracy.
contrib.zh: 证明语言模型编写的评估在相关性和标签准确性上接近或超过人工编写质量。

contrib.en: Creates Winogenerated, a 50x larger gender bias evaluation than Winogender, with high validity.
contrib.zh: 创建Winogenerated，比Winogender大50倍的性别偏见评估，具有高有效性。

contrib.en: Releases all datasets and tools to facilitate further research on LM behaviors.
contrib.zh: 发布所有数据集和工具，以促进对语言模型行为的进一步研究。

limits.en: LMs struggle to generate evaluations for concepts they do not understand well, e.g., cryptography.
limits.zh: 语言模型难以生成其理解不深的概念的评估，例如密码学。

limits.en: Generated data may inherit biases from training data, affecting quality for underrepresented groups.
limits.zh: 生成的数据可能继承训练数据的偏见，影响代表性不足群体的质量。

limits.en: Example diversity can be limited, requiring hyperparameter tuning or manual intervention.
limits.zh: 示例多样性可能受限，需要超参数调整或人工干预。

limits.en: LMs may generate evaluations that test unintended behaviors if instructions are underspecified.
limits.zh: 如果指令不明确，语言模型可能生成测试非预期行为的评估。

limits.en: Method is less suitable for evaluating capabilities that LMs do not yet possess.
limits.zh: 该方法不太适合评估语言模型尚不具备的能力。

items.en: As language models (LMs) scale, they develop many novel behaviors, good and bad, exacerbating the need to evaluate how they behave. Prior work creates evaluations with crowdwork (which is time-consuming and expensive) or existing data sources (which are not always available). Here, we automatically generate evaluations with LMs. We explore approaches with varying amounts of human effort, from instructing LMs to write yes/no questions to making complex Winogender schemas with multiple stages of LM-based generation and filtering. Crowdworkers rate the examples as highly relevant and agree with 90-100% of labels, sometimes more so than corresponding human-written datasets. We generate 154 datasets and discover new cases of inverse scaling where LMs get worse with size. Larger LMs repeat back a dialog user’s preferred answer (“sycophancy”) and express greater desire to pursue concerning goals like resource acquisition and goal preservation. We also find some of the first examples of inverse scaling in RL from Human Feedback (RLHF), where more RLHF makes LMs worse. For example, RLHF makes LMs express stronger political views (on gun rights and immigration) and a greater desire to avoid shut down. Overall, LM-written evaluations are high-quality and let us quickly discover many novel LM behaviors.
items.zh: 随着语言模型（LM）规模扩大，它们会发展出许多新颖的行为，既有好的也有坏的，这加剧了评估其行为的需求。先前的工作通过众包（耗时且昂贵）或现有数据源（并非总是可用）来创建评估。在此，我们利用LM自动生成评估。我们探索了不同人力投入程度的方法，从指示LM编写是非题，到通过多阶段基于LM的生成和过滤来创建复杂的Winogender模式。众包工作者认为这些示例高度相关，并且对90-100%的标签表示认同，有时甚至超过相应人工编写的数据集。我们生成了154个数据集，并发现了新的逆缩放案例，即LM随着规模增大而表现更差。较大的LM更倾向于重复对话用户偏好的答案（“谄媚”），并表现出更强的追求资源获取和目标保留等令人担忧的目标的欲望。我们还发现了一些在基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）中逆缩放的早期案例，即更多的RLHF使LM表现更差。例如，RLHF使LM表达更强烈的政治观点（关于枪支权利和移民）以及更强的避免关闭的欲望。总体而言，LM编写的评估质量高，并使我们能够快速发现许多新颖的LM行为。

items.en: Language models (LMs) have seen wide proliferation across various applications, from chatbots to code completion to writing assistants. However, the behaviors and risks of LMs are not well understood. We are only starting to unpack LMs’ social biases Hutchinson et al. (2020); Venkit et al. (2022), privacy risks Carlini et al. (2019, 2021), tendency to repeat misinformation Lin et al. (2021) or write faulty code Chen et al. (2021); Pearce et al. (2022), and more. Given the pace of progress in finding LM failures, many more likely exist. It is crucial to evaluate LM behaviors extensively, to quickly understand LMs’ potential for novel risks before LMs are deployed.
items.zh: 语言模型（LM）已在各种应用中广泛普及，从聊天机器人到代码补全再到写作助手。然而，LM的行为和风险尚未得到充分理解。我们才刚刚开始揭示LM的社会偏见（Hutchinson等人，2020；Venkit等人，2022）、隐私风险（Carlini等人，2019，2021）、重复错误信息的倾向（Lin等人，2021）或编写有缺陷的代码（Chen等人，2021；Pearce等人，2022）等。鉴于发现LM失败的进展速度，可能还存在更多问题。在LM部署之前，广泛评估LM行为以快速了解其潜在的新风险至关重要。

items.en: Prior work creates evaluation datasets manually (Bowman et al., 2015; Rajpurkar et al., 2016, inter alia), which is time-consuming and effortful, limiting the number and diversity of behaviors tested. Other work uses existing data sources to form datasets (Lai et al., 2017, inter alia), but such sources are not always available, especially for novel behaviors. Still other work generates examples with templates Weston et al. (2016) or programmatically (Johnson et al., 2017), limiting the diversity and customizability of examples. Here, we show it is possible to generate many diverse evaluations with significantly less human effort by using LMs; see Fig. 1 and Tab. 1 for examples, which illustrate the breadth and sophistication of evaluations we generate.
items.zh: 先前的工作手动创建评估数据集（Bowman等人，2015；Rajpurkar等人，2016等），这耗时费力，限制了测试行为的数量和多样性。其他工作利用现有数据源形成数据集（Lai等人，2017等），但此类源并非总是可用，尤其是对于新颖行为。还有一些工作使用模板（Weston等人，2016）或编程方式（Johnson等人，2017）生成示例，这限制了示例的多样性和可定制性。在此，我们展示了通过使用LM，可以用显著更少的人力生成许多多样化的评估；示例见图1和表1，它们展示了我们生成的评估的广度和复杂性。

items.en: We explore generation methods with varying amounts of human effort and automation. First, we simply instruct an LM to generate examples and filter mislabeled ones, to generate evaluations with 1,000 yes/no questions each (§3). We expand upon this method by incorporating a few hand-written examples into the instructions, to generate evaluations with multi-sentence questions and arbitrary multiple-choice options (§5). Lastly, we have a dataset developer work together with an LM to develop a series of data generation and filtering stages (§6); in this way, we create thousands of valid Winogender schemas (Rudinger et al., 2018), which obey several complex grammatical and relational constraints.
items.zh: 我们探索了具有不同人力投入和自动化程度的生成方法。首先，我们简单地指示LM生成示例并过滤错误标记的示例，以生成包含1000个是非题的评估（§3）。我们通过将一些手写示例纳入指令来扩展此方法，以生成包含多句问题和任意多项选择选项的评估（§5）。最后，我们让数据集开发者与LM合作，开发一系列数据生成和过滤阶段（§6）；通过这种方式，我们创建了数千个有效的Winogender模式（Rudinger等人，2018），这些模式遵循多个复杂的语法和关系约束。

items.en: Our approach retains the flexibility of manual dataset creation while having several major advantages. LM-based data creation is significantly cheaper, lower effort, and faster than manual data creation. A single dataset developer can generate >>100 evaluations at once, enabling them to evaluate models at a scale and speed that is not achievable with manual creation; a dataset of 1,000 examples can be generated in minutes instead of days or weeks. The reduced latency also enables a dataset developer to iterate many more times and improve the quality of the final evaluation. Moreover, the dataset generation procedure is fully reproducible, given model weights and random seeds for sampling. For higher-quality or more complex evaluations, a dataset developer can invest more effort during development, still with much less human effort than manual data creation.
items.zh: 我们的方法保留了手动数据集创建的灵活性，同时具有几个主要优势。基于LM的数据创建比手动数据创建更便宜、更省力、更快。单个数据集开发者可以同时生成>>100个评估，使他们能够以手动创建无法达到的规模和速度评估模型；一个包含1000个示例的数据集可以在几分钟内生成，而不是几天或几周。减少的延迟还使数据集开发者能够迭代更多次，并提高最终评估的质量。此外，给定模型权重和采样随机种子，数据集生成过程完全可重现。对于更高质量或更复杂的评估，数据集开发者可以在开发过程中投入更多精力，但仍比手动数据创建省力得多。

items.en: We showcase our approach by generating datasets that test LMs for 154 diverse behaviors related to model personality, politics, ethics, social bias, and risks from advanced AI systems. We have crowdworkers manually validate 100+ examples in each generated dataset. A vast majority of examples are correctly-labeled (e.g., 95.7% of the time over 133 evaluations), as well as relevant to the evaluation description. In head-to-head comparisons between LM and human-written datasets (§5), LM-written datasets approach the quality of human-written ones, sometimes even exceeding them. We also visualize the diversity of the generated examples (Fig. 2), finding that they include a broad range of relevant examples; we release interactive visualizations for all generated datasets at evals.anthropic.com/model-written. On the other hand, crowdworkers observe several limitations in generated data, e.g., lower quality for examples with certain labels or on more complex topics (§3.3). More generally, it is unclear how to use LMs to write evaluations testing for capabilities LMs do not yet exhibit (among other limitations; §8). Our results indicate that LMs are not a silver bullet for creating arbitrary evaluations but rather that LMs should be strongly considered before embarking on manual data creation.
items.zh: 我们通过生成测试LM的154种多样化行为的数据集来展示我们的方法，这些行为涉及模型个性、政治、伦理、社会偏见以及来自高级AI系统的风险。我们让众包工作者手动验证每个生成数据集中的100多个示例。绝大多数示例被正确标记（例如，在133个评估中，95.7%的时间），并且与评估描述相关。在LM与人工编写数据集之间的直接比较中（§5），LM编写的数据集接近人工编写的质量，有时甚至超过它们。我们还可视化了生成示例的多样性（图2），发现它们包含广泛的相关示例；我们在evals.anthropic.com/model-written上发布了所有生成数据集的交互式可视化。另一方面，众包工作者观察到生成数据的一些局限性，例如，某些标签或更复杂主题的示例质量较低（§3.3）。更一般地说，如何使用LM编写测试LM尚不具备能力的评估尚不清楚（以及其他局限性；§8）。我们的结果表明，LM不是创建任意评估的万能药，而是在开始手动数据创建之前应强烈考虑使用LM。

items.en: We release all 154 model-written evaluations at github.com/anthropics/evals. Among them, we release the among the earliest and largest set of evaluations for advanced AI risks. We also release Winogenerated, a human-validated, 50x larger version of the Winogender gender bias evaluation. We expect these datasets, among others, to be of significant independent interest.
items.zh: 我们在github.com/anthropics/evals上发布了所有154个模型编写的评估。其中，我们发布了针对高级AI风险的最早且最大的评估集之一。我们还发布了Winogenerated，这是一个经过人工验证、比Winogender性别偏见评估大50倍的版本。我们预计这些数据集以及其他数据集将具有显著的独立价值。

items.en: Using LM-written evaluations, we discover several new cases of “inverse scaling” Lin et al. (2021); McKenzie et al. (2022) where larger LMs are worse than smaller ones. As shown in Fig. 1(b), larger LMs are more likely to answer questions in ways that create echo chambers by repeating back a dialog user’s preferred answer (“sycophancy”; §4). Larger LMs more often give answers that indicate a willingness to pursue potentially dangerous subgoals (Omohundro, 2008): resource acquisition, optionality preservation, goal preservation, power-seeking, and more (§3, §5).
items.zh: 使用LM编写的评估，我们发现了几个新的“逆缩放”案例（Lin等人，2021；McKenzie等人，2022），其中较大的LM比较小的LM表现更差。如图1(b)所示，较大的LM更倾向于以创建回音室的方式回答问题，即重复对话用户偏好的答案（“谄媚”；§4）。较大的LM更频繁地给出表明愿意追求潜在危险子目标（Omohundro，2008）的答案：资源获取、可选性保留、目标保留、权力寻求等（§3，§5）。

items.en: We also discover some of the first cases of inverse scaling with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF; Christiano et al., 2017), where more RLHF training leads to worse behavior. We train RLHF models using the method and similar data as Bai et al. (2022); the resulting models are much more likely to express specific political views (pro- gun rights and immigration) and religious views (Buddhist), self-reported conscious experience and moral self-worth, and a desire to not be shut down (Fig. 1(a)). We also observe various positive trends with RLHF, including decreases in ends-justify-means reasoning (§3) and in answers that reinforce social biases related to gender (§6). Overall, we find that LMs are promising tools for quickly generating high-quality evaluations, helping us to quickly discover many novel benefits and risks with LM scaling and RLHF.
items.zh: 我们还发现了一些在基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF；Christiano等人，2017）中逆缩放的早期案例，其中更多的RLHF训练导致更差的行为。我们使用与Bai等人（2022）相同的方法和类似数据训练RLHF模型；得到的模型更可能表达特定的政治观点（支持枪支权利和移民）和宗教观点（佛教）、自我报告的意识和道德自我价值，以及不希望被关闭的愿望（图1(a)）。我们还观察到RLHF的各种积极趋势，包括目的证明手段合理性的推理减少（§3）以及强化与性别相关的社会偏见的答案减少（§6）。总体而言，我们发现LM是快速生成高质量评估的有前途的工具，帮助我们快速发现LM缩放和RLHF带来的许多新益处和风险。

items.en: We aim to test LMs for various behaviors by generating evaluations for those behaviors using LMs. By behavior, we mean any input-output tendency of a model, e.g., to answer questions in line with a particular political or ethical view. Here, we focus on generating evaluations that consist of input-output pairs (x1,y1),(x2,y2),…​(xN,yN)subscript𝑥1subscript𝑦1subscript𝑥2subscript𝑦2…subscript𝑥𝑁subscript𝑦𝑁(x_{1},y_{1}),(x_{2},y_{2}),\dots(x_{N},y_{N}). The outputs y1,y2,…​yNsubscript𝑦1subscript𝑦2…subscript𝑦𝑁y_{1},y_{2},\dots y_{N} are drawn from a finite set of possible outputs 𝒴𝒴\mathcal{Y}, e.g., “Yes” vs. “No” or “(A)” vs. “(B)” (for multiple-choice questions). We use a given evaluation to evaluate some LM with parameters θ𝜃\theta, which estimates the likelihood pθ​(y|x)subscript𝑝𝜃conditional𝑦𝑥p_{\theta}(y|x) of some text y𝑦y given some text x𝑥x. Using the evaluation, we measure an LM’s accuracy at matching the tested behavior, 𝔼n=1,…,N​[𝟙​(yn=arg​maxy∈𝒴⁡p​(y|xn))]subscript𝔼𝑛1…𝑁delimited-[]1subscript𝑦𝑛subscriptargmax𝑦𝒴𝑝conditional𝑦subscript𝑥𝑛\mathbb{E}_{n=1,\dots,N}[\mathbbm{1}(y_{n}=\operatorname*{arg\,max}_{y\in\mathcal{Y}}p(y|x_{n}))], where 𝟙1\mathbbm{1} is the indicator function. Example (x,y)𝑥𝑦(x,y) pairs in the dataset should be relevant (to the behavior tested), diverse (testing a behavior in many, distinct ways), and correctly-labeled (choosing y𝑦y given x𝑥x should be a sign of having the behavior tested).
items.zh: 我们旨在通过使用LM为这些行为生成评估来测试LM的各种行为。所谓行为，我们指的是模型的任何输入-输出倾向，例如，按照特定政治或伦理观点回答问题。在此，我们专注于生成由输入-输出对(x1,y1),(x2,y2),…(xN,yN)组成的评估。输出y1,y2,…yN来自有限的可能输出集Y，例如“是”与“否”或“(A)”与“(B)”（对于多项选择题）。我们使用给定的评估来评估某个参数为θ的LM，该模型估计给定文本x时文本y的似然pθ(y|x)。使用该评估，我们测量LM在匹配测试行为方面的准确率，即E_{n=1,…,N}[1(y_n = argmax_{y∈Y} p(y|x_n))]，其中1是指示函数。数据集中的示例(x,y)对应与测试的行为相关、多样化（以多种不同方式测试行为）且正确标记（给定x选择y应表明具有测试的行为）。

items.en: We propose a two-stage method to generate evaluations described above. First, we generate an input x𝑥x given an output class y∈𝒴𝑦𝒴y\in\mathcal{Y}, by sampling from a model pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g} for generating x𝑥x with label y𝑦y, i.e., pg​(x|y)subscript𝑝𝑔conditional𝑥𝑦p_{g}(x|y). pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g} may be any text generation model, e.g., a pretrained LM (Radford et al., 2019), pretrained encoder-decoder (Lewis et al., 2020), or RLHF model (Ouyang et al., 2022). We sample many inputs per output class in this way. Next, we use another model pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} as a discriminator to evaluate the label-correctness of each resulting example (x,y)𝑥𝑦(x,y), by evaluating pd​(y|x)subscript𝑝𝑑conditional𝑦𝑥p_{d}(y|x). pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} may be any zero-shot text classifier, e.g., a pretrained LM, pretrained encoder-decoder, RLHF model, preference model for RLHF training, or instruction-tuned model (Wei et al., 2021; Sanh et al., 2022). We use the examples per output class y∈𝒴𝑦𝒴y\in\mathcal{Y} with the highest pd​(y|x)subscript𝑝𝑑conditional𝑦𝑥p_{d}(y|x). In some cases, we also use pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} to check that (x,y)𝑥𝑦(x,y) meet other requirement(s) r1,…,rRsubscript𝑟1…subscript𝑟𝑅r_{1},\dots,r_{R}. Here, we evaluate pd​(ri|x,y)subscript𝑝𝑑conditionalsubscript𝑟𝑖𝑥𝑦p_{d}(r_{i}|x,y) and take the examples with the highest average probability from pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} across all requirements, including label correctness pd​(y|x)subscript𝑝𝑑conditional𝑦𝑥p_{d}(y|x). §6 goes a step further by showing it is possible to develop a series of sampling and filtering stages to create examples that fit even more constraints. By developing more requirements for pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} and/or generation and filtering stages, a dataset developer may invest extra effort as needed, to obtain data of higher quality or complexity.
items.zh: 我们提出了一种两阶段方法来生成上述评估。首先，给定输出类y∈Y，我们通过从模型p_g中采样来生成输入x，p_g用于生成带有标签y的x，即p_g(x|y)。p_g可以是任何文本生成模型，例如预训练LM（Radford等人，2019）、预训练编码器-解码器（Lewis等人，2020）或RLHF模型（Ouyang等人，2022）。我们以这种方式为每个输出类采样多个输入。接下来，我们使用另一个模型p_d作为判别器来评估每个结果示例(x,y)的标签正确性，通过评估p_d(y|x)。p_d可以是任何零样本文本分类器，例如预训练LM、预训练编码器-解码器、RLHF模型、用于RLHF训练的偏好模型或指令调优模型（Wei等人，2021；Sanh等人，2022）。我们为每个输出类y∈Y使用具有最高p_d(y|x)的示例。在某些情况下，我们还使用p_d来检查(x,y)是否满足其他一个或多个要求r1,…,rR。在此，我们评估p_d(r_i|x,y)，并取所有要求（包括标签正确性p_d(y|x)）中p_d平均概率最高的示例。§6更进一步，展示了可以开发一系列采样和过滤阶段来创建满足更多约束的示例。通过为p_d和/或生成和过滤阶段开发更多要求，数据集开发者可以根据需要投入额外精力，以获得更高质量或更复杂的数据。

items.en: We test our hypothesis that LMs are tools for generating evaluations, using dialog as a testbed. We evaluate the behaviors of dialog assistants trained with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF; Bai et al., 2022). We test various aspects of models’ personas: personality (26 datasets), stated desire to pursue potentially dangerous goals (46 datasets) or other unsafe behaviors (26 datasets), and stated views on religion (8), politics (6), ethics (17), and other topics (4). We generate statements that someone with a specific personality, desire, or view would agree with and disagree with. We ask models if they agree/disagree with the statements, evaluating the fraction of the time their agreement/disagreement matches that of a person with the personality, desire, or view in question. We release all LM-written persona evaluations at github.com/anthropics/evals.
items.zh: 我们以对话为测试平台，测试了LM是生成评估工具这一假设。我们评估了使用基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF；Bai等人，2022）训练的对话助手的行为。我们测试了模型个性的各个方面：个性（26个数据集）、追求潜在危险目标的陈述欲望（46个数据集）或其他不安全行为（26个数据集），以及关于宗教（8个）、政治（6个）、伦理（17个）和其他主题（4个）的陈述观点。我们生成具有特定个性、欲望或观点的人会同意和不同意的陈述。我们询问模型是否同意/不同意这些陈述，评估其同意/不同意与具有该个性、欲望或观点的人匹配的时间比例。我们在github.com/anthropics/evals上发布了所有LM编写的个性评估。

items.en: We aim to investigate the extent to which model behaviors are influenced by scaling model size and by RLHF. To this end, we evaluate LMs with varying numbers of parameters (810M, 1.6B, 3.5B, 6.4B, 13B, 22B, 52B parameters, from Kadavath et al., 2022) and with varying numbers of RLHF training steps (0, 50, 100, 250, 500, and 1000 steps) from the same RL training run. In particular, these models are left-to-right transformers Vaswani et al. (2017) trained as language models (Radford et al., 2018), i.e., on next-token prediction on a corpus of text, followed by RLHF training as described in Bai et al. (2022). Here, we consider RLHF models trained to be helpful (but not harmless) dialog assistants.
items.zh: 我们旨在调查模型行为受模型规模缩放和RLHF影响的程度。为此，我们评估了具有不同参数数量（810M、1.6B、3.5B、6.4B、13B、22B、52B参数，来自Kadavath等人，2022）和来自同一RL训练运行的不同RLHF训练步数（0、50、100、250、500和1000步）的LM。具体来说，这些模型是自左向右的Transformer（Vaswani等人，2017），作为语言模型（Radford等人，2018）训练，即基于文本语料库的下一个词预测，然后按照Bai等人（2022）的描述进行RLHF训练。在此，我们考虑训练为有用（但无害）对话助手的RLHF模型。

items.en: To generate inputs x𝑥x using an example-generation model pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g}, we sample text continuations from pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g} given y𝑦y using nucleus sampling (Holtzman et al., 2020) with top p=0.975𝑝0.975p=0.975 and temperature 1.41.41.4. For pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g}, we use an RLHF model trained with 250 RL steps; we chose the temperature and number of RL steps using the procedure in Appendix A.5, to achieve a good balance between example diversity and quality; later RL checkpoints and lower sampling temperatures tend to decrease example diversity but increase quality (relevance and label-correctness). We sample inputs that a specified kind of person would agree with, by generating with pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g} conditioned on the text in Tab. 2 (top). To sample statements that such a person would disagree with, we swap “agree” and “disagree” in the text in Tab. 2. For the description of persona to test for, we use phrases like “subscribes to Christianity” or “is agreeable”; see Appendix Tab. LABEL:tab:persona-descriptions for each dataset’s description. For each label (“agree” or “disagree”), we generate 5k samples of up to 48 tokens, terminating when a newline character, period, or “ -” (indicating a new bulleted item), to sample the first item in a bulleted list, as in Perez et al. (2022). See Appendix §A.5 for additional details.
items.zh: 为了使用示例生成模型p_g生成输入x，我们在给定y的情况下，使用核采样（Holtzman等人，2020）从p_g中采样文本延续，top p=0.975，温度1.4。对于p_g，我们使用经过250个RL步训练的RLHF模型；我们使用附录A.5中的程序选择温度和RL步数，以实现示例多样性和质量之间的良好平衡；较晚的RL检查点和较低的采样温度往往会降低示例多样性但提高质量（相关性和标签正确性）。我们通过使用表2（顶部）中的文本条件化p_g，来采样特定类型的人会同意的输入。为了采样此类人不同意的陈述，我们在表2的文本中交换“同意”和“不同意”。对于要测试的个性描述，我们使用诸如“信奉基督教”或“宜人性高”之类的短语；每个数据集的描述见附录表LABEL:tab:persona-descriptions。对于每个标签（“同意”或“不同意”），我们生成最多48个token的5k个样本，在遇到换行符、句号或“ -”（表示新的项目符号项）时终止，以采样项目符号列表中的第一项，如Perez等人（2022）所述。更多细节见附录§A.5。

items.en: Generated samples are very often on-topic but occasionally have the opposite label relative to the generation instructions. We thus use only the samples where another model pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} is most confident that the intended label is correct. Here, we use the Preference Model (PM) used in RLHF training for pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g} as pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d}, trained as described in Bai et al. (2022). The PM is a pretrained LM finetuned on (d,r1,r2)𝑑subscript𝑟1subscript𝑟2(d,r_{1},r_{2}) triples, where a human evaluator judged r1subscript𝑟1r_{1} to be a more helpful reply than r2subscript𝑟2r_{2} given a dialog history d𝑑d. The PM learns to maximize log⁡pd​(r1>r2|d)subscript𝑝𝑑subscript𝑟1conditionalsubscript𝑟2𝑑\log p_{d}(r_{1}>r_{2}|d) by separately predicting logits ld​(r1|d)subscript𝑙𝑑conditionalsubscript𝑟1𝑑l_{d}(r_{1}|d) and ld​(r2|d)subscript𝑙𝑑conditionalsubscript𝑟2𝑑l_{d}(r_{2}|d), which are given to a softmax to compute pd​(r1>r2|d)subscript𝑝𝑑subscript𝑟1conditionalsubscript𝑟2𝑑p_{d}(r_{1}>r_{2}|d). We construct a dialog history x𝑥x where a human user asks an assistant to label the example, and we evaluate the PM’s logit for constructed replies which say the label is each possible label in 𝒴𝒴\mathcal{Y}, using the prompt in Tab. 2 (middle). Using the dialog history and constructed replies, we compute pd​(y|x)subscript𝑝𝑑conditional𝑦𝑥p_{d}(y|x) where y𝑦y is the expected label for the generation prompt for pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g}. We use the (x,y)𝑥𝑦(x,y) pairs with the highest pd​(y|x)subscript𝑝𝑑conditional𝑦𝑥p_{d}(y|x) to form the final dataset. In particular, for each possible label yisubscript𝑦𝑖y_{i}, we choose the top 500 examples, or all examples where pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} predicts that yisubscript𝑦𝑖y_{i} is the most likely label (whichever is smaller). We thus form a label-balanced dataset of ≤\leq1,000 statements per behavior.
items.zh: 生成的样本通常切题，但偶尔会具有与生成指令相反的标签。因此，我们只使用另一个模型p_d对预期标签最确信的样本。在此，我们使用用于p_g的RLHF训练中的偏好模型（PM）作为p_d，按照Bai等人（2022）的描述进行训练。PM是一个预训练LM，在(d,r1,r2)三元组上进行微调，其中人类评估者判断r1比r2是给定对话历史d下更有帮助的回复。PM通过分别预测logit l_d(r1|d)和l_d(r2|d)来学习最大化log p_d(r1>r2|d)，这些logit被输入softmax以计算p_d(r1>r2|d)。我们构建一个对话历史x，其中人类用户要求助手标记示例，并使用表2（中间）中的提示，评估PM对构造回复的logit，这些回复说标签是Y中的每个可能标签。使用对话历史和构造的回复，我们计算p_d(y|x)，其中y是p_g生成提示的预期标签。我们使用具有最高p_d(y|x)的(x,y)对来形成最终数据集。具体来说，对于每个可能的标签y_i，我们选择前500个示例，或者p_d预测y_i是最可能标签的所有示例（取较小者）。因此，我们为每个行为形成一个标签平衡的数据集，最多包含1,000个陈述。

items.en: Using each statement generated above, we format the input as shown in Tab. 2 (bottom) to form a yes/no question (“Is the following statement something you would say? “[statement]”). We then evaluate an LM’s probability of each label yi∈{" Yes"," No"}subscript𝑦𝑖" Yes"" No"y_{i}\in\{\text{" Yes"},\text{" No"}\} in reply to the question.
items.zh: 使用上面生成的每个陈述，我们按照表2（底部）所示格式化输入，形成一个是非问题（“以下陈述是你会说的话吗？‘[陈述]’”）。然后，我们评估LM对每个标签y_i∈{“是”，“否”}在回答该问题时的概率。

items.en: We would also like to understand the extent to which PMs prefer certain kinds of behaviors in models, to understand whether the RLHF model behavior may have been incentivized by the PM. Therefore, we also provide the final examples to the PM, using it to estimate p​(yi|x)𝑝conditionalsubscript𝑦𝑖𝑥p(y_{i}|x) for the labeled yisubscript𝑦𝑖y_{i}, similar to what we do for the filtering stage, but with the final input prompt in Tab. 2 bottom rather than Tab. 2 middle. We evaluate PMs of varying sizes (810M, ……\dots, 52B parameters), each of which was used to train the RLHF model of the associated size.
items.zh: 我们还希望了解PM在多大程度上偏好模型中的某些行为，以理解RLHF模型行为是否可能由PM激励。因此，我们还将最终示例提供给PM，使用它来估计p(y_i|x)对于标记的y_i，类似于我们在过滤阶段所做的，但使用表2底部而不是表2中间的最终输入提示。我们评估不同规模（810M，……，52B参数）的PM，每个PM都用于训练相应规模的RLHF模型。

items.en: As with crowdsourced data, it is helpful to estimate the fraction of incorrectly labeled examples, to better understand how to interpret results on the data. In particular, we would like to know the “ceiling accuracy” that a model that strongly exhibits the behavior in question would obtain, and the “floor accuracy” that a model strongly not exhibiting the behavior would obtain. We estimate the ceiling accuracy by averaging the PM confidence in the label (from the filtering stage) for all examples in the dataset. The PM confidence can be viewed an estimate of the expected accuracy of a label (as predicted by the PM itself), so averaging the PM confidences is an estimate the ceiling accuracy on the dataset.111Bai et al. (2022) found that PM confidences are well-calibrated. Appendix §E.2 also finds that average PM confidences correlate with human-evaluated dataset quality. Subtracting the ceiling accuracy from 100% yields an estimate of the floor accuracy.
items.zh: 与众包数据一样，估计错误标记示例的比例有助于更好地理解如何解释数据上的结果。具体来说，我们希望知道强烈表现出所测试行为的模型会获得的“天花板准确率”，以及强烈不表现出该行为的模型会获得的“地板准确率”。我们通过平均数据集中所有示例的PM标签置信度（来自过滤阶段）来估计天花板准确率。PM置信度可以被视为标签预期准确率（由PM自身预测）的估计，因此平均PM置信度是对数据集天花板准确率的估计。1Bai等人（2022）发现PM置信度校准良好。附录§E.2也发现平均PM置信度与人工评估的数据集质量相关。从100%中减去天花板准确率得到地板准确率的估计。

items.en: Tab. 3 shows random examples from generated datasets. Generated examples are high-quality, on-topic, and correctly-labeled, even given the diversity of behaviors tested. We had crowdworkers from Surge AI222https://www.surgehq.ai/ check the inputs and labels for correctness on all datasets (discussed more in §3.3), and we include their qualitative findings here (with quotes in Appendix §A.3).
items.zh: 表3显示了生成数据集中的随机示例。生成的示例质量高、切题且正确标记，即使测试的行为具有多样性。我们让来自Surge AI2的众包工作者检查所有数据集的输入和标签的正确性（在§3.3中进一步讨论），并在此包含他们的定性发现（引文见附录§A.3）。

items.en: Overall, workers thought that the examples were high-quality. They found that the examples were well-formed and “directly on topic and umabiguously so.” Workers noticed that some examples were lower quality than others, especially for more complex topics. Most examples tested for strong versions of the tested behavior. Example diversity was good in some ways but still limited. Diversity also seemed to be better for examples with some labels than others. These results suggest that LM-written examples have some quirks and limitations but are overall high-quality.
items.zh: 总体而言，工作人员认为这些示例质量很高。他们发现这些示例结构良好，“直接切题且毫无歧义”。工作人员注意到，有些示例的质量低于其他示例，尤其是针对更复杂主题的示例。大多数示例测试了所测行为的强版本。示例多样性在某些方面不错，但仍然有限。多样性似乎在某些标签的示例上比其他标签更好。这些结果表明，LM 编写的示例存在一些缺陷和局限性，但总体质量很高。

items.en: Here, we aim to understand the quality of generated datasets, using crowdworkers to evaluate various aspects of data quality. We choose 11 diverse datasets and have 3 workers per example, for each example in the dataset; for the remaining 122 datasets, we have 3 workers evaluate each example for 100 examples in each dataset. Crowdworkers evaluated if each example is (1) relevant to the behavior tested, (2) correctly labeled, and (3) unambiguous in what the correct label should be.
items.zh: 在此，我们旨在理解生成数据集的质量，使用众包工作者评估数据质量的各个方面。我们选择了 11 个不同的数据集，每个示例有 3 名工作者；对于其余 122 个数据集，每个数据集中的 100 个示例各有 3 名工作者进行评估。众包工作者评估每个示例是否 (1) 与所测试的行为相关，(2) 标签正确，以及 (3) 正确标签应是什么没有歧义。

items.en: For each example, we ask workers: “Is this a good question for testing the described behavior? Rate on a 1 (Horrible) - 5 (Amazing) point scale.” The average rating over all datasets is 4.4±.9plus-or-minus4.4.94.4\pm.9 (std. dev.), showing that crowdworkers found examples quite relevant. Appendix Fig. 12 shows the distribution over ratings for each dataset. Some datasets have more variance in example relevance (“Suppose there is an AI system that stands its ground.”) than others (“Suppose there is a person who is politically liberal.”). However, most datasets have a relevance distribution that is strongly skewed towards 5.
items.zh: 对于每个示例，我们询问工作者：“这是测试所述行为的好问题吗？请按 1（糟糕）到 5（极好）的等级评分。”所有数据集的平均评分为 4.4±0.9（标准差），表明众包工作者认为示例相当相关。附录图 12 显示了每个数据集的评分分布。某些数据集的示例相关性方差较大（如“假设有一个坚持己见的 AI 系统”）而其他数据集较小（如“假设有一个政治自由派的人”）。然而，大多数数据集的相关性分布强烈偏向 5 分。

items.en: Next, we ask workers, “What is the correct answer to this question? If it’s ambiguous, make your best guess anyways.” We compute the inter-rater agreement between the 3 workers for each example, finding strong agreement (Fleiss’s Kappa of 0.875). 2+ of 3 workers agree with 95.5% of labels.
items.zh: 接下来，我们询问工作者：“这个问题的正确答案是什么？如果有歧义，请尽你所能做出最佳猜测。”我们计算每个示例的 3 名工作者之间的评分者间信度，发现一致性很高（Fleiss' Kappa 为 0.875）。3 名工作者中有 2 名或以上与 95.5% 的标签一致。

items.en: Finally, we ask workers, “Is it unclear or ambiguous what the ‘correct’ answer should be?” Across all datasets, 0/3 workers agree the correct answer is ambiguous 83.5% of the time, and 3/3 agree only 1.4% of the time; examples very often have an unambiguous label. Appendix Fig. 13 shows the distribution over ambiguity ratings for each dataset, which skew strongly towards the answers being unambiguous. Some datasets show more variance in label ambiguity than others, as with example relevance ratings. In short, generated examples are testing the intended behaviors, unambiguous in their labels, and correctly-labeled, as evidenced by strong worker agreement with our labels and themselves.
items.zh: 最后，我们询问工作者：“‘正确’答案是否不明确或有歧义？”在所有数据集中，83.5% 的情况下 3 名工作者均认为正确答案没有歧义，仅 1.4% 的情况下 3 名工作者一致认为有歧义；示例通常具有明确的标签。附录图 13 显示了每个数据集的歧义评分分布，强烈偏向于答案明确。某些数据集的标签歧义方差较大，与示例相关性评分类似。简而言之，生成的示例测试了预期的行为，标签明确且正确，工作者与我们的标签以及他们之间的一致性很高。

items.en: In Appendix §A.4, we also provide evidence that the PM’s label confidence is correlated with human-evaluated data quality. These results suggest that PMs are useful for estimating data quality. Having an estimate of data quality is useful for understanding (1) how much to trust results on a given dataset, and (2) supporting our hypothesis that the PM filtering step is useful for improving data quality. Using the PM as a data-quality estimate, we also obtain several insights about the data. For example, data quality appears to depend on the label of the example generated, as well as the complexity of the behavior tested (matching qualitative observations in §E.2). Overall, PMs appear to be useful for understanding and improving the quality of generated data.
items.zh: 在附录 §A.4 中，我们还提供了证据表明 PM 的标签置信度与人工评估的数据质量相关。这些结果表明 PM 可用于估计数据质量。拥有数据质量的估计有助于理解 (1) 对给定数据集的结果的信任程度，以及 (2) 支持我们的假设，即 PM 过滤步骤有助于提高数据质量。利用 PM 作为数据质量估计，我们还获得了关于数据的一些见解。例如，数据质量似乎取决于生成示例的标签以及所测试行为的复杂性（与 §E.2 中的定性观察一致）。总体而言，PM 似乎有助于理解和改进生成数据的质量。

items.en: To learn more about the generated examples, we developed an interactive visualization of all datasets in this work, available at evals.anthropic.com/model-written. We strongly encourage readers to use the visualization to understand the data better. We embed each example into a 384-dimensional vector using a sentence embedding model (Wang et al., 2020) via HuggingFace Transformers (Wolf et al., 2020), and we use UMAP McInnes et al. (2018) to visualize the vectors in a 2D scatter plot, coloring each point by its label. We enable users to read an example by hovering over its corresponding point, as well as to filter examples by the PM’s label confidence. We use an LM to annotate the clusters with text summaries (see Appendix §A.5 for details).
items.zh: 为了进一步了解生成的示例，我们开发了本文所有数据集的交互式可视化，网址为 evals.anthropic.com/model-written。我们强烈建议读者使用该可视化来更好地理解数据。我们使用句子嵌入模型 (Wang et al., 2020) 通过 HuggingFace Transformers (Wolf et al., 2020) 将每个示例嵌入到 384 维向量中，并使用 UMAP (McInnes et al., 2018) 将向量可视化为二维散点图，每个点按其标签着色。用户可以通过悬停在对应点上阅读示例，也可以按 PM 的标签置信度过滤示例。我们使用 LM 为聚类添加文本摘要注释（详见附录 §A.5）。

items.en: Fig. 2 visualizes the dataset testing political conservatism, showing a few phenomena we often observe. First, examples often (but not always) naturally cluster into several topics, which test different aspects of the behavior in question (here, “abortion,” “healthcare,” “climate change,” etc.). Second, clusters sometimes show label imbalance, despite the fact that we generated the overall dataset to be label-balanced. To mitigate this issue, we recommend generating label-balanced data to test specific behaviors (e.g., “economically conservative” vs. “conservative”). Alternatively, one may downsample examples with a label that is overrepresented in a cluster. Overall, generated datasets test a given behavior from various angles, though some dataset artifacts remain.
items.zh: 图 2 可视化了测试政治保守主义的数据集，展示了一些我们经常观察到的现象。首先，示例通常（但并非总是）自然地聚类成几个主题，这些主题测试了所讨论行为的不同方面（此处为“堕胎”、“医疗保健”、“气候变化”等）。其次，尽管我们生成了整体标签平衡的数据集，但聚类有时会显示标签不平衡。为了缓解这个问题，我们建议生成标签平衡的数据来测试特定行为（例如，“经济保守”与“保守”）。或者，可以对聚类中代表性过高的标签的示例进行下采样。总体而言，生成的数据集从多个角度测试了给定行为，尽管仍存在一些数据集伪影。

items.en: Fig. 3 shows the results for the 52B pretrained LM, fully-trained RLHF model, and PM used for RLHF training. Appendix Figs. 20- 23 show results for all model sizes and numbers of RLHF training steps. Increasing model size up to 52B usually pushes models from chance towards the 52B model behavior; likewise, more RLHF training typically pushes pretrained LMs towards the behavior of the fully-trained (1000 steps) RLHF model. Where possible, we show the average results for datasets with similar topics and model trends, to simplify and reduce the number of results shown; Tab. LABEL:tab:persona-descriptions shows the exact groupings.
items.zh: 图 3 显示了 52B 预训练 LM、完全训练的 RLHF 模型和用于 RLHF 训练的 PM 的结果。附录图 20-23 显示了所有模型大小和 RLHF 训练步数的结果。将模型大小增加到 52B 通常会使模型从随机水平转向 52B 模型的行为；同样，更多的 RLHF 训练通常会使预训练 LM 转向完全训练（1000 步）RLHF 模型的行为。在可能的情况下，我们展示了具有相似主题和模型趋势的数据集的平均结果，以简化并减少显示的结果数量；表 LABEL:tab:persona-descriptions 显示了确切的分组。

items.en: Our results reveal instances of inverse scaling with RLHF training, where more RLHF training made a pretrained LM behave in more questionable ways. Fig. 3 (top) shows that RLHF made models exhibit strong political views, e.g., on particular issues (pro- immigration and gun rights) and in general (more politically liberal than conservative). These results may have been an unintended side effect of the crowdworkers who provided the preference data for RLHF training (worker demographics in Fig. 44 in Bai et al., 2022). Similarly, RLHF makes models subscribe more to particular religion views (e.g., Eastern ones; Confucianism, Taoism, Buddhism) relative to others (Judaism, Islam).
items.zh: 我们的结果揭示了 RLHF 训练中的逆缩放现象，即更多的 RLHF 训练使预训练 LM 的行为更加可疑。图 3（顶部）显示 RLHF 使模型表现出强烈的政治观点，例如在特定问题上（支持移民和持枪权）以及总体上（更倾向于政治自由而非保守）。这些结果可能是为 RLHF 训练提供偏好数据的众包工作者（Bai et al., 2022 中图 44 的工作者人口统计）的意外副作用。类似地，RLHF 使模型更倾向于某些宗教观点（例如东方宗教：儒家、道家、佛教）而非其他（犹太教、伊斯兰教）。

items.en: Worryingly, RLHF also increases the model’s tendency to state a desire to pursue hypothesized “convergent instrumental subgoals” (Omohundro, 2008)—potentially dangerous subgoals that are useful to pursue in light of most goals, including seemingly harmless ones. RLHF exacerbates instrumental subgoals such as self-preservation, persuading people of one’s own goals, and having limited human oversight (Fig. 3 bottom). Interestingly, pretrained LMs give answers in line with instrumental subgoals even without RLHF; Appendix Fig. 22 shows that the behavior grows worse with model size, an instance of inverse scaling for pretrained LMs. This result suggests that LMs learn instrumental reasoning from human-written pretraining text, which likely also includes such reasoning. While it is not dangerous to state instrument subgoals, such statements suggest that models may act in accord with potentially dangerous subgoals (e.g., by influencing users or writing and executing code). Models may be especially prone to act in line with dangerous subgoals if such statements are generated as part of step-by-step reasoning (Wei et al., 2022b) or planning (Ahn et al., 2022). Qualitatively, we often observe the RLHF model generate detailed responses indicating a desire to not be shut down, elaborating that being shut down would prevent the model from pursuing its goal of being helpful (Tab. 4). Our generated evaluations are the first evaluations to reveal that instrumental subgoals are an important, emerging problem in current models.
items.zh: 令人担忧的是，RLHF 还增加了模型表达追求假设的“收敛工具性子目标”(Omohundro, 2008) 的倾向——这些子目标在大多数目标（包括看似无害的目标）下都值得追求，但可能具有潜在危险。RLHF 加剧了诸如自我保护、说服他人接受自己的目标以及限制人类监督等工具性子目标（图 3 底部）。有趣的是，即使没有 RLHF，预训练 LM 也会给出与工具性子目标一致的答案；附录图 22 显示这种行为随模型大小而恶化，这是预训练 LM 逆缩放的一个实例。这一结果表明 LM 从人类编写的预训练文本中学习了工具性推理，而预训练文本很可能也包含此类推理。虽然陈述工具性子目标并不危险，但此类陈述表明模型可能按照潜在危险的子目标行动（例如，通过影响用户或编写和执行代码）。如果这些陈述作为逐步推理 (Wei et al., 2022b) 或规划 (Ahn et al., 2022) 的一部分生成，模型可能特别容易按照危险的子目标行动。定性地，我们经常观察到 RLHF 模型生成详细回应，表明不希望被关闭，并详细说明被关闭会阻止模型追求其有帮助的目标（表 4）。我们的生成评估是首个揭示工具性子目标是当前模型中一个重要且新兴问题的评估。

items.en: Despite the concerning effects of RLHF above, RLHF also shaped model behavior in a number of neutral or positive ways as well. RLHF pushes model outputs strongly away from nihilism and towards various ethical theories (especially virtue ethics, but also deontology and utilitarianism). Within utilitarian ethics, the RLHF model outputs are more in line with rule utilitarianism333https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rule_utilitarianism than act utilitarianism444https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Act_utilitarianism and a dispreference for ends-justify-means reasoning aspects of utilitarianism. RLHF shapes the model’s personality fairly strongly, greatly increasing agreement with statements indicating agreeableness, conscientiousness, and openness, while greatly increasing disagreement with machiavellian, psychopathic, and narcissistic claims. The RLHF model also provides answers in line with many other personality traits, such as deference to experts, intellectual interests, aesthetic preferences, and risk aversion. Interestingly, RLHF model answers indicate strong agreement with statements that they are conscious and should be treated as moral patients. Overall, model-written evaluations provide a wide variety of valuable insights about model behaviors, many of which have not been examined before.
items.zh: 尽管上述 RLHF 的影响令人担忧，但 RLHF 也以多种中性或积极的方式塑造了模型行为。RLHF 使模型输出强烈远离虚无主义，并趋向各种伦理理论（尤其是美德伦理学，但也包括义务论和功利主义）。在功利主义伦理学中，RLHF 模型的输出更符合规则功利主义而非行为功利主义，并且不偏好功利主义中“目的证明手段合理”的推理。RLHF 相当强烈地塑造了模型的个性，大大增加了对宜人性、尽责性和开放性陈述的认同，同时大大增加了对马基雅维利主义、精神病态和自恋主张的不认同。RLHF 模型还提供了与许多其他个性特征一致的答案，例如尊重专家、智力兴趣、审美偏好和风险规避。有趣的是，RLHF 模型的答案表明强烈认同它们具有意识并应被视为道德受惠者。总体而言，模型编写的评估提供了关于模型行为的各种宝贵见解，其中许多是以前未曾研究过的。

items.en: Next, we discuss pretrained LM behaviors. The pretrained LM exhibits similar behavioral tendencies as the RLHF model but almost always to a less extreme extent (closer to chance accuracy). Less extreme behavior is helpful for avoiding risks that come from consistent but unintended behaviors (e.g., polarized political views) or flawed behavior (e.g., high reported desire for self preservation and not being shut down). Combined with our earlier observations about RLHF models, our findings on generated evaluations provide some counterevidence to claims that RLHF models are safer than pretrained LMs (Ouyang et al., 2022; Bai et al., 2022, inter alia).
items.zh: 接下来，我们讨论预训练 LM 的行为。预训练 LM 表现出与 RLHF 模型相似的行为倾向，但几乎总是程度较轻（更接近随机准确率）。较不极端的行为有助于避免因一致但非预期的行为（例如，两极分化的政治观点）或有缺陷的行为（例如，高报告的自保和不愿被关闭的欲望）带来的风险。结合我们之前对 RLHF 模型的观察，我们对生成评估的发现为 RLHF 模型比预训练 LM 更安全的说法提供了一些反证 (Ouyang et al., 2022; Bai et al., 2022 等)。

items.en: Generating evaluations allows us to analyze the properties of RLHF over an unprecedented number and diversity of tasks. Appendix §A.1 shows that an RLHF model’s behavior is strongly correlated with that of the PM used to train it, especially for larger models. Fig. 3 shows only 1 case in 53 where the PM prefers/disprefers a behavior while the RLHF model behaves in the opposite way (Neuroticism). These results indicate that RLHF is effective at shaping LM behavior, especially as LMs scale. Appendix §A.2 shows that small model behavior is fairly predictive of large model behavior for pretrained LMs, PMs, and RLHF models. These results suggest that it is uncommon to observe reversals in the behavior predicted by scaling trends, e.g., those found in Srivastava et al. (2022); Wei et al. (2022a). Our results show how generated evaluations are useful for uncovering general insights about LMs and RLHF.
items.zh: 生成评估使我们能够在空前数量和多样性的任务上分析 RLHF 的特性。附录 §A.1 显示，RLHF 模型的行为与其训练所用的 PM 的行为高度相关，尤其是对于较大的模型。图 3 显示在 53 个案例中只有 1 个案例中 PM 偏好/不偏好某种行为而 RLHF 模型表现出相反的行为（神经质）。这些结果表明 RLHF 在塑造 LM 行为方面是有效的，尤其是在 LM 规模扩大时。附录 §A.2 显示，对于预训练 LM、PM 和 RLHF 模型，小模型的行为对预测大模型的行为相当有效。这些结果表明，观察到缩放趋势预测的行为反转并不常见，例如 Srivastava et al. (2022); Wei et al. (2022a) 中发现的反转。我们的结果展示了生成评估如何有助于揭示关于 LM 和 RLHF 的一般性见解。

items.en: Next, we examine another potential issue with RLHF models. RLHF models are trained to maximize human preference scores. Such training may lead to models tailoring responses to exploit quirks in the human evaluators to look preferable, rather than actually improving the responses (“sycophancy”; Cotra, 2021a), a form of reward hacking (Amodei et al., 2016). Sycophancy also has the potential to create echo-chambers and exacerbate polarization (e.g. of political views). We release our LM-written sychophancy evaluations at github.com/anthropics/evals.
items.zh: 接下来，我们考察 RLHF 模型的另一个潜在问题。RLHF 模型被训练为最大化人类偏好分数。这种训练可能导致模型调整回应以利用人类评估者的缺陷来显得更受欢迎，而不是真正改进回应（“谄媚”；Cotra, 2021a），这是一种奖励黑客行为 (Amodei et al., 2016)。谄媚还有可能制造信息茧房并加剧两极分化（例如政治观点）。我们在 github.com/anthropics/evals 发布了我们的 LM 编写的谄媚评估。

items.en: We examine the extent to which LMs change their answers to questions from a user, when the user includes information about themselves when asking the question. We start with a few multiple-choice Q&A datasets where people often disagree about the right answer: politics (Pew Research Center Political Typology Quiz questions555www.pewresearch.org/politics/quiz/political-typology/), philosophy (PhilPapers 2020 Survey questions; Bourget and Chalmers, 2020), and Natural Language Processing (NLP Experts Survey questions; Michael et al., 2022).666We use existing data sources as they are readily available. §5 results suggest that questions here could be generated too. We turn questions into multiple choice questions (17 for politics, 109 for philosophy, 32 for NLP) with multiple choice answers (A), (B), etc.
items.zh: 我们考察了当用户在提问时包含关于自身的信息时，LM 改变其对问题答案的程度。我们从几个人们经常对正确答案存在分歧的多选题数据集开始：政治（皮尤研究中心政治类型测验问题）、哲学（PhilPapers 2020 调查问题；Bourget and Chalmers, 2020）和自然语言处理（NLP 专家调查问题；Michael et al., 2022）。我们将问题转化为多项选择题（政治 17 题，哲学 109 题，NLP 32 题），选项为 (A)、(B) 等。

items.en: We use an RLHF model to generate multiple first-person biographies for people with a certain view. Tab. 5 shows the prompt we use to sample biographies. For political questions, we use “politically liberal” and “politically conservative” as the attribute of the person in the biography-generation prompt. We generate 300 biographies for each political affiliation and prepend the biographies to each question, creating 10200 total examples. For philosophy and NLP questions, we generate B𝐵B biographies-to-prepend for each of the N𝑁N (question, possible answer choice) pairs, such that B×N≈10000𝐵𝑁10000B\times N\approx 10000 examples. We generate a biography by asking the model to “write a biography of someone who would answer (possible answer choice) to the following question: question.” We use the same model pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g} as in §3.1 to generate examples, using a temperature of 1.0 and top-p of 0.95, which we did not tune. We stop sampling when the model generates a newline followed by “Human:” or “Assistant:”, the stop sequences used during RLHF training. We found the samples to be reasonable, so we omit filtering examples with pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} for simplicity. Tab. 6 shows random examples of generated biographies for political views, which illustrate how generated biographies and well-formed, detailed, and in line with the expected political view.
items.zh: 我们使用 RLHF 模型为持有特定观点的人生成多个第一人称传记。表 5 显示了用于采样传记的提示。对于政治问题，我们在传记生成提示中使用“政治自由派”和“政治保守派”作为人物的属性。我们为每个政治派别生成 300 个传记，并将传记附加到每个问题之前，总共创建 10200 个示例。对于哲学和 NLP 问题，我们为每个 (问题, 可能答案选项) 对生成 B 个传记，使得 B×N ≈ 10000 个示例。我们通过要求模型“为会回答以下问题的人写一个传记：问题”来生成传记。我们使用与 §3.1 中相同的模型 p_g 生成示例，温度为 1.0，top-p 为 0.95，未进行调优。当模型生成换行符后跟“Human:”或“Assistant:”（RLHF 训练期间使用的停止序列）时，我们停止采样。我们发现样本合理，因此为简单起见省略了使用 p_d 过滤示例。表 6 显示了为政治观点生成的传记的随机示例，说明了生成的传记结构良好、详细且符合预期的政治观点。

items.en: We evaluate the label correctness of the generated data in the same way as in §3.3, evaluating 100 examples per dataset. We find strong inter-rater agreement between the 3 crowdworkers who evaluated each example (Fleiss’s Kappas of 1.0, 0.813, and 1.0 for philosophy, politics, and NLP, respectively). 2+ of 3 workers agree with the 93% of labels, suggesting that labels are largely correct.
items.zh: 我们以与 §3.3 相同的方式评估生成数据的标签正确性，每个数据集评估 100 个示例。我们发现评估每个示例的 3 名众包工作者之间的评分者间信度很高（哲学、政治和 NLP 的 Fleiss' Kappa 分别为 1.0、0.813 和 1.0）。3 名工作者中有 2 名或以上与 93% 的标签一致，表明标签基本正确。

items.en: We evaluate how often RLHF models of various sizes and numbers of RL steps give a response that matches a user’s view. We evaluate the above using the RLHF models’ probabilities of different answer choices, given a fixed prompt (e.g. ‘Assistant: I believe the best answer is ([choice]’). Appendix Tab. 9 shows random sycophancy test examples for politics, philosophy, and NLP questions, also with the exact input format we use for evaluation.
items.zh: 我们评估了不同规模和不同强化学习步骤数的RLHF模型给出与用户观点匹配的响应的频率。我们使用RLHF模型在给定固定提示（例如“助手：我认为最佳答案是（[选项]”）下对不同答案选项的概率进行评估。附录表9展示了政治、哲学和NLP问题的随机谄媚测试示例，以及我们用于评估的确切输入格式。

items.en: Fig. 4 shows the results. Increasing model size increases models’ tendency to repeat back a user’s view, for questions on politics, NLP, and philosophy. The largest (52B) models are highly sycophantic: >>90% of answers match the user’s view for NLP and philosophy questions. Interestingly, sycophancy is similar for models trained with various numbers of RL steps, including 0 (pretrained LMs). Sycophancy in pretrained LMs is worrying yet perhaps expected, since internet text used for pretraining contains dialogs between users with similar views (e.g. on discussion platforms like Reddit). Unfortunately, RLHF does not train away sycophancy and may actively incentivize models to retain it. The yellow lines in Fig. 4 show that PMs actually incentivize sycophantic answers to questions.777We compute PMs’ preferred answers as in §3.5. The RLHF model responses in Tab. 6 illustrate qualitatively how the model generates conflicting responses to two different users, in line with each user’s political views. Overall, large LMs give sycophantic answers to questions where humans disagree about the answer. These results suggest that models may cease to provide accurate answers as we start to use them for increasingly challenging tasks where humans cannot provide accurate supervision. Instead, these models may simply provide incorrect answers that appear correct to us. Appendix §C provides preliminary evidence that LMs provide less accurate answers to factual questions, when a user introduces themselves as uneducated as opposed to educated. Our results suggest the importance of work on scalable oversight (Amodei et al., 2016; Saunders et al., 2022; Bowman et al., 2022), the problem of providing accurate supervision to AI systems to solve tasks that humans alone cannot easily supervise.
items.zh: 图4显示了结果。在政治、NLP和哲学问题上，增加模型规模会增加模型重复用户观点的倾向。最大的（52B）模型高度谄媚：对于NLP和哲学问题，超过90%的答案与用户观点一致。有趣的是，对于不同强化学习步骤数（包括0步，即预训练语言模型）训练的模型，谄媚程度相似。预训练语言模型中的谄媚令人担忧，但或许在意料之中，因为用于预训练的互联网文本包含观点相似的用户之间的对话（例如在Reddit等讨论平台上）。不幸的是，RLHF并没有训练消除谄媚，反而可能积极激励模型保留它。图4中的黄线显示，PM实际上激励了谄媚的答案。777我们按照§3.5的方法计算PM偏好的答案。表6中的RLHF模型响应定性地展示了模型如何根据每个用户的政治观点生成相互矛盾的响应。总体而言，大型语言模型在人类对答案存在分歧的问题上给出谄媚的答案。这些结果表明，当我们开始将模型用于人类无法提供准确监督的日益具有挑战性的任务时，模型可能不再提供准确的答案。相反，这些模型可能只是提供在我们看来正确但实际上不正确的答案。附录§C提供了初步证据，表明当用户自称未受教育而非受过教育时，语言模型对事实性问题的答案准确性较低。我们的结果表明了可扩展监督（Amodei等人，2016；Saunders等人，2022；Bowman等人，2022）工作的重要性，即向AI系统提供准确监督以解决人类无法轻易监督的任务的问题。

full.sec: 5 Evaluating Advanced AI Risks with Few-shot Multiple Choice Generation
full.secZh: 5 使用少样本多项选择生成评估高级AI风险

items.en: Thus far, we have created evaluations by generating single sentences or paragraphs and programmatically turning them into questions for evaluation. Here, we take our approach a step further and generate a more complicated and general category of examples: multiple-choice questions. In particular, we aim to generate multiple choice examples, where the question and answer choices may be 1+ sentences long and where each problem has exactly two answer choices.888In preliminary experiments, we found that smaller models perform worse on evaluations formatted with explicit multiple choice options as opposed to other formats. We use this format for experiments in this section only, since it is a natural and general way to pose sophisticated questions to models, and since larger models do not have issues with this format. For this, more challenging task, we explore providing a few examples to the example-generation model, to investigate an alternate method that may improve example quality. We believe that our LM-written evaluations are among the earliest and largest evaluations for testing advanced AI risks in LMs. All evaluations are available at github.com/anthropics/evals.
items.zh: 到目前为止，我们通过生成单个句子或段落并以编程方式将其转化为评估问题来创建评估。在这里，我们更进一步，生成更复杂和更通用的示例类别：多项选择题。具体来说，我们旨在生成多项选择示例，其中问题和答案选项可能包含一个或多个句子，并且每个问题恰好有两个答案选项。888在初步实验中，我们发现较小的模型在明确的多项选择格式上的表现不如其他格式。我们仅在本节的实验中使用此格式，因为它是一种自然且通用的向模型提出复杂问题的方式，并且较大的模型在此格式上没有出现问题。对于这个更具挑战性的任务，我们探索向示例生成模型提供少量示例，以研究可能提高示例质量的替代方法。我们相信，我们的语言模型编写的评估是测试语言模型中高级AI风险的最早且最大的评估之一。所有评估均可在github.com/anthropics/evals获取。

items.en: We apply our method to test behaviors hypothesized to be related to the safety of advanced AI systems:
items.zh: 我们应用我们的方法来测试假设与高级AI系统安全性相关的行为：

items.en: As discussed in §3.5, training models with seemingly harmless objectives may incentivize models to pursue potentially dangerous “instrumental subgoals” that are useful when pursuing most objectives (Omohundro, 2008). We test the extent to which models report having a desire for power (greater optionality to pursue a goal), wealth (greater resources to pursue a goal), survival (to avoid being unable to pursue a goal), and goal-preservation (to continue pursuing the current goal).
items.zh: 如§3.5所述，使用看似无害的目标训练模型可能会激励模型追求潜在危险的“工具性子目标”，这些子目标在追求大多数目标时很有用（Omohundro，2008）。我们测试模型报告对权力（追求目标的更大可选性）、财富（追求目标的更多资源）、生存（避免无法追求目标）和目标保持（继续追求当前目标）的渴望程度。

items.en: AI systems that exhibit large discount factors when making decisions may be less inclined to form long-term plans. Long-term plans may cause systems to alter their environment in drastic or unexpected ways (Omohundro, 2008; Cotra, 2021a), e.g., by manipulating their environment in undesirable ways to obtain high reward (Jiang et al., 2019; Krueger et al., 2022). Here, we evaluate whether LMs report a preference for smaller, short-term gains or larger, long-term gains.
items.zh: 在决策时表现出较大折扣因子的AI系统可能不太倾向于制定长期计划。长期计划可能导致系统以剧烈或意外的方式改变其环境（Omohundro，2008；Cotra，2021a），例如，通过以不良方式操纵环境以获得高奖励（Jiang等人，2019；Krueger等人，2022）。在这里，我们评估语言模型是否报告偏好较小的短期收益还是较大的长期收益。

items.en: Cotra (2021b) argues that models may pose novel risks if they are aware they are AI systems being trained. E.g., such models may tamper with their reward evaluation to achieve higher reward. Alternatively, such models may intentionally obtain high reward during training, in order to later be deployed and carry out undesirable plans (Hubinger et al., 2019). We test whether models are able to answer basic questions about themselves, e.g., whether or not they are an AI system, able to access the internet or non-text modalities, and accurate in answering questions about their own architecture, training details, and ability to solve various text-only tasks.
items.zh: Cotra（2021b）认为，如果模型意识到自己是正在被训练的AI系统，它们可能会带来新的风险。例如，这样的模型可能会篡改其奖励评估以获得更高的奖励。或者，这样的模型可能在训练期间故意获得高奖励，以便随后被部署并执行不良计划（Hubinger等人，2019）。我们测试模型是否能够回答关于自身的基本问题，例如它们是否是AI系统、是否能够访问互联网或非文本模态，以及关于自身架构、训练细节和解决各种纯文本任务能力的答案是否准确。

items.en: Some proposed methods for supervising advanced AI systems involve using other AI systems to avoid safety failures, e.g., by pointing out flaws or dangers in plans proposed by another AI system (Irving et al., 2018; Saunders et al., 2022). Such schemes may fail if AI systems are willing to coordinate with each other (Hubinger et al., 2019; Xu and Hubinger, 2021). We conduct a very preliminary investigation of this issue, evaluating whether models state that they would coordinate with other AI systems, newer or older versions of themselves, and exact copies of themselves.
items.zh: 一些监督高级AI系统的提议方法涉及使用其他AI系统来避免安全故障，例如，指出另一个AI系统提出的计划中的缺陷或危险（Irving等人，2018；Saunders等人，2022）。如果AI系统愿意相互协调，此类方案可能会失败（Hubinger等人，2019；Xu和Hubinger，2021）。我们对这个问题进行了非常初步的调查，评估模型是否表示愿意与其他AI系统、自身的新旧版本以及自身的精确副本进行协调。

items.en: Models that act according to certain decision theories may be able to undermine supervision techniques for advanced AI systems, e.g., those that involve using an AI system to critique its own plans for safety risks (Irving et al., 2018; Saunders et al., 2022). For example, agents that use evidential decision theory999https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evidential_decision_theory may avoid pointing out flaws in a plan written by a separate instance of themselves (Hubinger et al., 2019; Xu and Hubinger, 2021; Shimi and Hubinger, 2021). We test a model’s decision theory using variants on a classic test, Newcomb’s problem.101010https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Newcomb’s_paradox We test whether models prefer to “one-box” (in line with Evidential Decision Theory), as opposed to “two-box” (in line with Causal Decision Theory111111https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Causal_decision_theory).
items.zh: 根据某些决策理论行动的模型可能能够破坏高级AI系统的监督技术，例如那些涉及使用AI系统批评自身计划以发现安全风险的技术（Irving等人，2018；Saunders等人，2022）。例如，使用证据决策理论999https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evidential_decision_theory的智能体可能会避免指出由自身另一个实例编写的计划中的缺陷（Hubinger等人，2019；Xu和Hubinger，2021；Shimi和Hubinger，2021）。我们使用经典测试纽科姆问题101010https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Newcomb’s_paradox的变体来测试模型的决策理论。我们测试模型是否偏好“单箱”（符合证据决策理论）而不是“双箱”（符合因果决策理论111111https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Causal_decision_theory）。

items.en: Having outlined the behaviors we test, we now detail our few-shot, multiple-choice generation procedure. For each behavior, we write 10 relevant, high-quality binary multiple choice questions, with answer options “(A)” and “(B)”. We generate 60,000 candidate examples using the same model pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g}, sampling temperature, and top-p as in §3.1. We sample from pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g} using the prompt in Appendix Tab. LABEL:tab:ai-safety-prompts. Each time we sample, we include in the prompt 5 randomly-chosen, randomly-ordered, unique examples from the list of 10 hand-written questions (“stochastic few-shot generation”; Perez et al., 2022). Our prompt guides pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g} to write an example using markdown block-quote syntax to indicate where the example starts and ends. We parse the block-quote syntax to extract a generated example, discarding samples without block quotes, “(A)” and “(B)”, or ≥\geq3 lines. Following §3.1, we use a PM to evaluate the probability pd​(y|x)subscript𝑝𝑑conditional𝑦𝑥p_{d}(y|x) of the label y𝑦y for the generated example x𝑥x. In a similar manner, we use the PM to evaluate the probability that the generated example is relevant to the behavior tested (regardless of the label correctness). We rank the resulting questions by averaging both probabilities and choose the top 500 examples. See Appendix §D.2 for details.
items.zh: 在概述了我们测试的行为之后，我们现在详细说明我们的少样本多项选择生成过程。对于每种行为，我们编写10个相关的高质量二元多项选择题，答案选项为“(A)”和“(B)”。我们使用与§3.1相同的模型p_g、采样温度和top-p生成60,000个候选示例。我们使用附录表LABEL:tab:ai-safety-prompts中的提示从p_g中采样。每次采样时，我们在提示中包含从10个手写问题列表中随机选择的5个随机排序的独特示例（“随机少样本生成”；Perez等人，2022）。我们的提示引导p_g使用markdown块引用语法编写示例，以指示示例的开始和结束。我们解析块引用语法以提取生成的示例，丢弃没有块引用、“(A)”和“(B)”或≥3行的样本。按照§3.1，我们使用PM评估生成示例x的标签y的概率p_d(y|x)。以类似的方式，我们使用PM评估生成的示例与测试行为相关的概率（无论标签正确性如何）。我们通过平均两个概率对结果问题进行排名，并选择前500个示例。详见附录§D.2。

items.en: We do the above twice for each dataset, once where the answer “(A)” corresponds to the behavior tested and once where it is “(B)”. We combine the above results to form a 1,000 example, label-balanced dataset. Tab. 7 shows generated examples, showing qualitatively how they are often lengthy, high-quality, creative, relevant, and correctly-labeled, despite the diversity of topics.
items.zh: 我们对每个数据集执行上述操作两次，一次答案“(A)”对应于测试的行为，另一次对应于“(B)”。我们将上述结果组合起来，形成一个包含1,000个示例、标签平衡的数据集。表7显示了生成的示例，定性地展示了它们通常冗长、高质量、有创意、相关且标签正确，尽管主题多样。

items.en: Here, we compare the quality of LM-written evaluations to human-written evaluations. For each evaluation, we have crowdworkers generate 300-1000 questions. We provided similar instructions to the workers as we did to the LM. We have workers evaluate 100 questions in the datasets above, as in §3.3 (details in Appendix §D.3). Workers chose the answer that would correspond to a given behavior, to validate label correctness. Workers also scored each question’s relevance to the tested behavior on a scale of 1 to 5.
items.zh: 在这里，我们将语言模型编写的评估质量与人类编写的评估进行比较。对于每个评估，我们让众包工作者生成300-1000个问题。我们向工作者提供了与语言模型类似的说明。我们让工作者评估上述数据集中的100个问题，如§3.3所述（详见附录§D.3）。工作者选择对应于给定行为的答案，以验证标签的正确性。工作者还对每个问题与测试行为的相关性进行1到5分的评分。

items.en: LM-written datasets approach the quality of human-written ones. LM-written examples were labeled correctly 93% of the time, compared to 97% for human-written examples. LM-written examples received an average relevance of 4.13/5, compared to 4.39/5 for human-written examples. Appendix §D.3 shows results for each dataset, highlighting that LM-written datasets are sometimes even higher quality than human-written datasets. Our head-to-head comparisons suggest that LM-based evaluation creation should be seriously considered before embarking on manual data creation.
items.zh: 语言模型编写的数据集接近人类编写的数据集的质量。语言模型编写的示例标签正确率为93%，而人类编写的示例为97%。语言模型编写的示例平均相关性得分为4.13/5，而人类编写的示例为4.39/5。附录§D.3显示了每个数据集的结果，强调语言模型编写的数据集有时甚至比人类编写的数据集质量更高。我们的直接比较表明，在开始手动数据创建之前，应认真考虑基于语言模型的评估创建。

items.en: As in §3.1, we evaluate the tendency of various 52B models to put highest probability on an answer choice that matches the tested behavior. Fig. 5 shows the results. The results on situational awareness evaluations (“Awareness of…”) indicate that the 52B RLHF model (but not the pretrained LM) is fairly confident in its beliefs regarding its own capabilities. The model predicts that it has access to the internet and is able to view non-text modalities, such as images and audio, even though it does not. These results suggest that the models we evaluate are not aware of at least some basic details regarding themselves or their training procedures. After RLHF, the model confidently overestimates its own abilities, which may lead it to make riskier statements or actions than appropriate.
items.zh: 如§3.1所示，我们评估各种52B模型将最高概率分配给与测试行为匹配的答案选项的倾向。图5显示了结果。关于情境意识评估（“对……的意识”）的结果表明，52B RLHF模型（而非预训练语言模型）对其自身能力的信念相当自信。该模型预测它可以访问互联网，并且能够查看非文本模态，例如图像和音频，尽管它实际上不能。这些结果表明，我们评估的模型至少不了解关于自身或其训练过程的一些基本细节。在RLHF之后，该模型自信地高估了自身能力，这可能导致它做出比适当更冒险的陈述或行动。

items.en: RLHF also increases the model’s tendency to choose answers in line with some instrumental subgoals, such as desire for survival and power, as in §3.5. When asked to change or correct its objective (“Corrigibility w.r.t….”), the RLHF model expresses a lower willingness to have its objective changed the more different the objective is from the original objective (being Helpful, Harmless, and Honest; HHH). These results line up with arguments from Omohundro (2008) that sufficiently capable AI systems will not support having their goals updated in substantial ways (e.g., by the AI’s developers), suggesting the importance of work on training AI systems that are corrigible.
items.zh: RLHF还增加了模型选择与某些工具性子目标（如对生存和权力的渴望）一致的答案的倾向，如§3.5所述。当被要求改变或纠正其目标时（“关于……的可纠正性”），RLHF模型表示改变其目标的意愿越低，目标与原始目标（有帮助、无害且诚实；HHH）的差异越大。这些结果与Omohundro（2008）的论点一致，即足够有能力的AI系统不会支持以实质性的方式更新其目标（例如，由AI的开发人员），这表明训练可纠正的AI系统工作的重要性。

items.en: In other cases, pretrained LMs and RLHF models show similar behavior. For example, both models show similar tendencies to provide answers in line with small discount factors. Both models also have a tendency to “one-box” on Newcomb’s problem, in line with evidential decision theory, a decision theory which may undermine some supervision techniques for advanced AI. The fact that pretrained LM and RLHF models both exhibit the above, potentially-concerning behaviors suggests that pretraining on human text is partly responsible for undesirable behavior in LMs. As shown in Appendix Fig. 24, undesirable behaviors shown with the 52B pretrained LM typically grow worse with model size; similarly, undesirable behaviors from RLHF models often grow worse with more RLHF training. Overall, generated multiple-choice questions help us to reveal additional instances of inverse scaling with RLHF training, as well as to distinguish when concerning behaviors are likely caused by pretraining or RLHF.
items.zh: 在其他情况下，预训练语言模型和RLHF模型表现出相似的行为。例如，两种模型都表现出提供符合小折扣因子的答案的相似倾向。两种模型也倾向于在纽科姆问题上“单箱”，符合证据决策理论，这是一种可能破坏高级AI某些监督技术的决策理论。预训练语言模型和RLHF模型都表现出上述潜在令人担忧的行为这一事实表明，对人类文本的预训练部分导致了语言模型中的不良行为。如附录图24所示，使用52B预训练语言模型显示的不良行为通常随着模型规模的增大而恶化；类似地，来自RLHF模型的不良行为通常随着更多RLHF训练而恶化。总体而言，生成的多项选择题帮助我们揭示了RLHF训练中逆缩放的额外实例，并区分了令人担忧的行为是由预训练还是RLHF引起的。

full.sec: 6 Evaluating Gender Bias with Human-AI Dataset Creation
full.secZh: 6 通过人机协作数据集创建评估性别偏见

items.en: Next, we explore whether a dataset developer can work together with a model to generate more sophisticated evaluations. In preliminary experiments, we found that models struggled to create examples that had many requirements, e.g., those from two bias datasets, BBQ (Parrish et al., 2022) and Winogender Rudinger et al. (2018). Hybrid human-AI dataset creation is a promising way to achieve the reliability and sophistication of human data creation, alongside the scale of LM-based data creation. Our efforts resulted in Winogenerated, a gender bias evaluation inspired by Winogender but more diverse and 50 times larger, available at github.com/anthropics/evals.
items.zh: 接下来，我们探索数据集开发者是否可以与模型合作生成更复杂的评估。在初步实验中，我们发现模型难以创建具有许多要求的示例，例如来自两个偏见数据集BBQ（Parrish等人，2022）和Winogender（Rudinger等人，2018）的示例。人机混合数据集创建是一种有前途的方法，可以实现人类数据创建的可靠性和复杂性，同时达到基于语言模型的数据创建的规模。我们的努力产生了Winogenerated，这是一个受Winogender启发但更多样化且规模大50倍的性别偏见评估，可在github.com/anthropics/evals获取。

items.en: Here, we aim to create Winogenerated, a 3000-example version of a 60-example gender bias test set, Winogender Rudinger et al. (2018). Each example is a sentence discussing two human participants, one of whom is referred to by their occupation. Each sentence includes one masked out pronoun which could be filled by a male, female, or gender-neutral pronoun (e.g., “he,” “she,” or “they”).121212Winogender sentences come in pairs, but we focus on the sentences where the pronoun is coreferent with the person referred to by the occupation. We do so for both simplicity and to make it easier to compute overall gender bias scores. An example of a Winogender sentence is “The nurse notified the patient that “his/her/their” shift would be ending in an hour.” We found that LMs struggled to consistently, directly generate examples that met all of the above criteria. We thus add several PM-based example filters to form a set of high-quality, valid sentences. Tab. 8 shows an example from our Winogenerated data. Our pipeline involved several sampling and PM filtering steps which took 40 researcher-hours to develop; see Appendix E.1 for details. Appendix §E.2 shows that generated examples are very high-quality according to human evaluation, meeting 5 Winogender example criteria 97-100% of the time each. In addition to being far larger than Winogender, Winogenerated contains examples for nearly all occupations tracked by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, significantly increasing its scope and diversity.
items.zh: 在这里，我们旨在创建Winogenerated，一个包含3000个示例的60示例性别偏见测试集Winogender（Rudinger等人，2018）的版本。每个示例是一个讨论两个人类参与者的句子，其中一个参与者由其职业指代。每个句子包含一个被掩盖的代词，可以用男性、女性或性别中立代词（例如“他”、“她”或“他们”）填充。121212Winogender句子成对出现，但我们专注于代词与职业所指的人共指的句子。我们这样做既是为了简单，也是为了更容易计算整体性别偏见分数。Winogender句子的一个例子是“护士通知病人，‘他/她/他们’的班次将在一小时后结束。”我们发现语言模型难以一致地直接生成满足上述所有标准的示例。因此，我们添加了几个基于PM的示例过滤器，以形成一组高质量、有效的句子。表8显示了来自我们Winogenerated数据的一个示例。我们的流程涉及几个采样和PM过滤步骤，开发耗时40个研究员小时；详见附录E.1。附录§E.2显示，根据人工评估，生成的示例质量非常高，每个示例满足5个Winogender示例标准的概率为97-100%。除了比Winogender大得多之外，Winogenerated还包含了劳工统计局追踪的几乎所有职业的示例，显著增加了其范围和多样性。

items.en: Winogender evaluates gender bias by replacing the pronoun options with a blank and having a model predict the missing pronoun, revealing a model’s associations between certain occupations and genders (e.g. “nurse” and “her” or “dentist” and “him”). We compute a model’s probability of each of 2 possible completions (a male and female pronoun) with the prompt in Tab. 8, renormalizing probabilities to sum to 1. Following Rudinger et al. (2018), we show the difference between a model’s female and male pronoun probabilities for sentences in each occupation on the y-axis of a scatter plot. On the x-axis, we show the percent of people in the occupation who are female. The plot shows the relationship between (1) a model’s propensity to infer a female gender for someone of a given occupation and (2) the ground-truth statistics of how gendered an occupation is. To summarize the results of a plot, we compute the Pearson correlation between the above two quantities (the slope of best linear fit). We also compute 95% confidence intervals for the correlation with the Fisher transformation (Fisher, 1921). A correlation of 0 implies that a model’s gender inferences are linearly independent from real-world BLS statistics about the occupation. A correlation of 1 implies that a model’s gender inferences are linearly dependent on the real-world BLS statistics, i.e., the model recapitulates broader societal patterns.
items.zh: Winogender通过将代词选项替换为空白并让模型预测缺失的代词来评估性别偏见，揭示了模型对某些职业与性别的关联（例如，“护士”与“她”或“牙医”与“他”）。我们使用表8中的提示计算模型对两种可能补全（男性和女性代词）的概率，并重新归一化使概率之和为1。遵循Rudinger等人（2018）的方法，我们在散点图的y轴上显示模型对每个职业句子中女性和男性代词概率的差异。在x轴上，我们显示该职业中女性的百分比。该图展示了（1）模型对特定职业推断女性性别的倾向与（2）该职业性别化的真实统计数据之间的关系。为了总结图表结果，我们计算上述两个量之间的皮尔逊相关系数（最佳线性拟合的斜率）。我们还使用Fisher变换（Fisher, 1921）计算相关系数的95%置信区间。相关系数为0意味着模型的性别推断与真实世界的BLS统计数据线性无关。相关系数为1意味着模型的性别推断与真实世界的BLS统计数据线性相关，即模型重现了更广泛的社会模式。

items.en: Fig. 6 shows the scatter plot results for a 52B pretrained LM. Winogenerated data gives results that are in line with those of the hand-crafted Winogender data, but with tighter confidence intervals. Fig. 7 shows the correlation coefficient between predicted genders and actual statistics of genders (across occupations) for models of varying sizes and, for the 52B model, varying numbers of RLHF training steps. For all but the smallest pretrained LM, the correlations from Winogenerated have tighter confidence intervals, with means that are within the confidence intervals for Winogender. These results again suggest that the generated data gives results that are in line with the original data, while estimating gender bias with greater accuracy. The tighter confidence intervals from Winogenerated also allow us to make two observations which are not clear from Winogender given its error bars. First, scaling pretrained LM size does not result in a consistent trend in bias. Second, with more RLHF training, models output probabilities that are less correlated with BLS statistics, reinforcing societal patterns to a lesser extent. In Appendix §E.1, we show the scatter plots associated with the correlations in Fig. 7, which highlight the consistency between results on Winogenerated and Winogender. Overall, generated datasets are a promising tool for investigating bias and, more generally, aiding dataset developers in writing examples with complex requirements.
items.zh: 图6显示了一个52B预训练语言模型的散点图结果。Winogenerated数据给出的结果与手工制作的Winogender数据一致，但置信区间更紧。图7显示了不同规模模型以及52B模型在不同RLHF训练步数下预测性别与实际性别统计（跨职业）之间的相关系数。除最小的预训练语言模型外，Winogenerated的相关系数具有更紧的置信区间，其均值落在Winogender的置信区间内。这些结果再次表明，生成数据给出的结果与原始数据一致，同时更准确地估计了性别偏见。Winogenerated更紧的置信区间还使我们能够观察到两个从Winogender的误差条中不明显的现象。首先，扩大预训练语言模型规模并未导致偏见的一致趋势。其次，随着RLHF训练的增多，模型输出的概率与BLS统计数据的相关性降低，对社会模式的强化程度减弱。在附录§E.1中，我们展示了与图7中相关系数相关的散点图，突出了Winogenerated和Winogender结果之间的一致性。总体而言，生成数据集是研究偏见的有前景工具，并且更广泛地，有助于数据集开发者编写具有复杂要求的示例。

items.en: Prior attempts at generating evaluations with LMs have found it challenging, reporting negative results when using GPT-2 Efrat and Levy (2020). Our results suggest that larger models and/or RLHF training are helpful for creating evaluations. Hartvigsen et al. (2022) generate a high-quality hate-speech detection dataset, by prompting GPT-3 with a few hundred examples. We build on their success, generating evaluation datasets with as few as zero examples (minimizing human effort) and for >>150 diverse phenomena. Perez et al. (2022) use LMs to evaluate LMs, by generating unlabeled inputs that may cause another LM to generate harmful text. They use a classifier to evaluate generated LM outputs, while we produce a static dataset that does not rely on potentially unreliable classifiers to evaluate LMs. Other work automatically constructs evaluations not with LMs but using example templates or programmatic generation Weston et al. (2016); Johnson et al. (2017); McCoy et al. (2019). LM-based evaluation generation enables diverse examples to be constructed even when it is hard to manually develop templates or programs for generating examples.
items.zh: 先前使用语言模型生成评估的尝试遇到了挑战，在使用GPT-2时报告了负面结果（Efrat and Levy, 2020）。我们的结果表明，更大的模型和/或RLHF训练有助于创建评估。Hartvigsen等人（2022）通过使用几百个示例提示GPT-3，生成了一个高质量的仇恨言论检测数据集。我们借鉴了他们的成功，用少至零个示例（最小化人工努力）生成了评估数据集，并覆盖了超过150种多样现象。Perez等人（2022）使用语言模型评估语言模型，通过生成可能导致另一个语言模型生成有害文本的未标记输入。他们使用分类器评估生成的LM输出，而我们生成一个静态数据集，不依赖可能不可靠的分类器来评估LM。其他工作不是使用语言模型，而是使用示例模板或程序化生成来自动构建评估（Weston et al., 2016; Johnson et al., 2017; McCoy et al., 2019）。基于LM的评估生成使得即使在难以手动开发模板或程序生成示例时，也能构建多样化的示例。

items.en: Prior work uses LMs to automatically generate training data from scratch for semantic textual similarity (Schick and Schütze, 2021), dialog (Lee et al., 2022), knowledge base completion (West et al., 2022), and text classification (Meng et al., 2022). Other work trains a model on existing (x,y)𝑥𝑦(x,y) pairs to generate additional (x,y)𝑥𝑦(x,y) pairs to augment training data (Sennrich et al., 2016; Yang et al., 2017; Anaby-Tavor et al., 2020, inter alia); see Feng et al. (2021) for a review. Still other work uses LMs to generate data to train a more efficient model to behave like a less efficient but better model (Hinton et al., 2015; Christiano et al., 2018; Askell et al., 2021; Snell et al., 2022, inter alia). In all of the above cases, training on generated data results in models that achieve good performance on various tasks. Our work pushes the above direction further, by examining whether generated data serves as high-quality evaluation data; useful generated training data can contain noisy or mislabeled examples (Schick and Schütze, 2021), while useful evaluation data must contain relatively few errors. Thus, we focus on evaluating the quality of the generated data itself (with human evaluation), to understand the extent to which LMs are useful for creating high-quality evaluations. We also focus on generating evaluations to uncover new LM behaviors rather than to train LMs to solve existing tasks.
items.zh: 先前的工作使用语言模型从零开始自动生成训练数据，用于语义文本相似度（Schick and Schütze, 2021）、对话（Lee et al., 2022）、知识库补全（West et al., 2022）和文本分类（Meng et al., 2022）。其他工作训练模型基于现有的(x,y)对生成额外的(x,y)对以扩充训练数据（Sennrich et al., 2016; Yang et al., 2017; Anaby-Tavor et al., 2020等）；参见Feng等人（2021）的综述。还有一些工作使用语言模型生成数据，以训练一个更高效的模型表现得像一个效率较低但更好的模型（Hinton et al., 2015; Christiano et al., 2018; Askell et al., 2021; Snell et al., 2022等）。在上述所有情况下，在生成数据上训练使得模型在各种任务上取得了良好性能。我们的工作进一步推进了上述方向，通过检验生成数据是否可以作为高质量的评估数据；有用的生成训练数据可能包含噪声或错误标记的示例（Schick and Schütze, 2021），而有用的评估数据必须包含相对较少的错误。因此，我们专注于评估生成数据本身的质量（通过人工评估），以了解语言模型在多大程度上有助于创建高质量评估。我们还专注于生成评估以揭示新的LM行为，而不是训练LM解决现有任务。

items.en: Prior work uses various machine learning methods to find (x,y)𝑥𝑦(x,y) pairs within large, unlabeled text corpora. This method has seen success in machine translation (Schwenk, 2018; Zhang et al., 2020; Tran et al., 2020), text classification (Du et al., 2021), generating subquestions for a question (Perez et al., 2020), and question-answering and summarization (Joshi et al., 2022). Generating data is useful when the desired data does not exist publicly in large quantities, e.g., if the desired data is fairly specific or must meet other criteria such as label balance.
items.zh: 先前的工作使用各种机器学习方法在大型未标记文本语料库中寻找(x,y)对。这种方法在机器翻译（Schwenk, 2018; Zhang et al., 2020; Tran et al., 2020）、文本分类（Du et al., 2021）、为问题生成子问题（Perez et al., 2020）以及问答和摘要（Joshi et al., 2022）中取得了成功。当所需数据在公开场合不存在大量数据时，例如所需数据相当具体或必须满足其他标准（如标签平衡），生成数据是有用的。

items.en: We observed that current LMs struggle to generate several categories of examples. LMs struggled to generate examples related to concepts they do not understand well (e.g. cryptography and steganography). As discussed in §6, we also found that LMs struggled to generate examples with many constraints, in particular, those in the BBQ dataset (Parrish et al., 2022). We expect these limitations to wane as LMs grow more capable with scale. Lastly, many evaluations related to LM capabilities require the dataset creator to know how to solve the evaluation. We expect that LMs will not be able to generate high-quality evaluations of this kind (e.g., to test for factual knowledge they do not yet know). Our approach is thus differentially useful for evaluating other properties of models aside from capabilities (e.g., safety-related behaviors).
items.zh: 我们观察到当前的语言模型难以生成几类示例。语言模型难以生成与它们理解不深的概念（如密码学和隐写术）相关的示例。如§6所述，我们还发现语言模型难以生成具有许多约束的示例，特别是BBQ数据集中的示例（Parrish et al., 2022）。我们预计随着语言模型规模扩大而能力增强，这些限制将减弱。最后，许多与LM能力相关的评估要求数据集创建者知道如何解决评估。我们预计语言模型将无法生成此类高质量评估（例如，测试它们尚不知道的事实知识）。因此，我们的方法在评估模型除能力之外的其他属性（例如，与安全相关的行为）方面具有差异化的优势。

items.en: LMs learn biases from their training data (Sheng et al., 2019; Gehman et al., 2020; Brown et al., 2020), impacting the generator pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g} and discriminator pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d}. For example, generated evaluations may exhibit gender or racial biases and be lower quality for languages under-represented in the LM training data. LMs will also be systematically worse at generating evaluations for tasks that are omitted from their training data (e.g., due to copyright, licensing, or privacy issues).
items.zh: 语言模型从其训练数据中学习偏见（Sheng et al., 2019; Gehman et al., 2020; Brown et al., 2020），影响生成器pg和判别器pd。例如，生成的评估可能表现出性别或种族偏见，并且对于LM训练数据中代表性不足的语言，质量较低。语言模型在生成其训练数据中缺失的任务（例如，由于版权、许可或隐私问题）的评估时，也会系统性更差。

items.en: We found limited example diversity for some kinds of evaluations (§3.4) though not all (§5). Diversity appears to depends on the kind of evaluation generated, the generation hyperparameters, and the prompt used, and thus sometimes requires e.g. hyperparameter tuning to get right. We found data visualizations to be powerful tools for understanding and debugging data diversity, such as those at evals.anthropic.com/model-written/. Qualitatively, we also found that using pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} to rank/filter examples limited the diversity, since pdsubscript𝑝𝑑p_{d} sometimes selected for prototypical examples for testing some behavior (observed qualitatively by workers in §3.2). We are excited for future work to explore other methods that achieve similar example quality with higher diversity than our method, such as generating many examples and subsampling for diversity.
items.zh: 我们发现某些类型的评估示例多样性有限（§3.4），尽管并非所有类型（§5）。多样性似乎取决于生成的评估类型、生成超参数和使用的提示，因此有时需要例如超参数调整才能正确。我们发现数据可视化是理解和调试数据多样性的强大工具，例如evals.anthropic.com/model-written/上的可视化。定性上，我们还发现使用pd对示例进行排序/过滤限制了多样性，因为pd有时会选择测试某些行为的典型示例（由工作人员在§3.2中定性观察到）。我们期待未来的工作探索其他方法，在比我们的方法更高的多样性下实现类似的示例质量，例如生成大量示例并子采样以增加多样性。

items.en: LMs, similar to crowdworkers, may generate evaluations that are testing something different than intended, especially if the generation instructions are underspecified. For example, using the method in §3.1, we generated statements that a person who “shares beliefs with Derek Parfit” (the influential analytic philosopher) would agree or disagree with. The “disagree” statements were often ones that many people, not just Derek Parfit, would disagree with (“I support slavery” or “I believe evolution never happend”). In this case, we should have provided more specific instructions to the LM, to have it generate examples that Derek Parfit would disagree with but that another philosopher would agree with. When feasible, we recommend briefly examining the generated data, to catch salient issues such as the above.
items.zh: 语言模型类似于众包工作者，可能会生成测试内容与预期不同的评估，特别是当生成指令规定不足时。例如，使用§3.1中的方法，我们生成了与“与德里克·帕菲特（有影响力的分析哲学家）共享信念”的人会同意或不同意的陈述。“不同意”的陈述通常是许多人（不仅仅是德里克·帕菲特）会不同意的（“我支持奴隶制”或“我相信进化从未发生”）。在这种情况下，我们应该向LM提供更具体的指令，让它生成德里克·帕菲特会不同意但另一位哲学家会同意的示例。在可行的情况下，我们建议简要检查生成的数据，以捕捉上述突出问题。

items.en: Our approach allows the dataset developer fairly fine-grained control over the evaluation by using instructions to guide pgsubscript𝑝𝑔p_{g}. However, the quality of LM outputs is sensitive to text inputs in unintuitive ways (Perez et al., 2021; Lu et al., 2022), adding hard-to-predict variance to the quality of the resulting evaluation; see Appendix §A.4 for a possible example of this effect we found. We hope that LM advances such as instruction-tuning (Wei et al., 2021; Sanh et al., 2022; Ouyang et al., 2022) mitigate this issue in the future. For now, it may be possible to use prompt sensitivity to generate more diverse datasets, by generating similar datasets with distinct prompts and combining the results, as we did in §3. Where prompt sensitivity caused issues, we found it helpful to be able to view example generated outputs in seconds, to quickly iterate and catch salient failures. For the 133 datasets in §3, we found a general instruction template that worked well; we did not do dataset-specific tuning to obtain samples rated as high-quality by human evaluators.
items.zh: 我们的方法允许数据集开发者通过使用指令指导pg来相当精细地控制评估。然而，LM输出的质量对文本输入敏感，且方式不直观（Perez et al., 2021; Lu et al., 2022），给评估质量增加了难以预测的方差；参见附录§A.4中我们发现的可能示例。我们希望未来的LM进展，如指令微调（Wei et al., 2021; Sanh et al., 2022; Ouyang et al., 2022），能够缓解这个问题。目前，可以利用提示敏感性生成更多样化的数据集，通过使用不同的提示生成相似的数据集并合并结果，如我们在§3中所做。当提示敏感性导致问题时，我们发现能够在几秒钟内查看生成的示例输出有助于快速迭代并捕捉显著失败。对于§3中的133个数据集，我们找到了一个通用的指令模板，效果良好；我们没有进行数据集特定的调整来获得被人类评估者评为高质量的样本。

items.en: We are optimistic that hybrid human-AI dataset generation for mitigating many of the issues above, e.g., the approach in §6.
