For example, it is possible to generate many (possibly flawed) examples with LMs and edit or filter them manually. Hybrid approaches have succeeded in generating evaluation data (Wu et al., 2021; Ross et al., 2021), training data (Liu et al., 2022), and adversarial training data (Nie et al., 2020; Xu et al., 2021; Bartolo et al., 2020, 2021; Ziegler et al., 2022).
items.zh: 我们对混合人机数据集生成以缓解上述许多问题持乐观态度，例如§6中的方法。例如，可以用LM生成许多（可能有缺陷的）示例，并手动编辑或过滤。混合方法在生成评估数据（Wu et al., 2021; Ross et al., 2021）、训练数据（Liu et al., 2022）和对抗训练数据（Nie et al., 2020; Xu et al., 2021; Bartolo et al., 2020, 2021; Ziegler et al., 2022）方面取得了成功。

items.en: We focus on generating classification-style evaluations, but many evaluations require text generation (e.g., to evaluate text summarization). For text generation evaluations, we recommend the related approach of Perez et al. (2022); Zhang et al. (2022), who generate inputs and evaluate LM outputs using an LM-based classifier.
items.zh: 我们专注于生成分类式评估，但许多评估需要文本生成（例如，评估文本摘要）。对于文本生成评估，我们推荐Perez等人（2022）和Zhang等人（2022）的相关方法，他们生成输入并使用基于LM的分类器评估LM输出。

items.en: Our results suggests that malicious actors may be able to use LMs to evaluate LMs’ tendencies to act in harmful ways, to exacerbate such tendencies. For example, a malicious actor may evaluate LMs for their tendency to persuade people towards their own political views, in order to influence the public’s views towards their own. Another issue is that our method is potentially useful to adversaries in finding and exploiting weaknesses in existing models (e.g., to circumvent product safety measures like safety filters131313i.e., the content filters from the OpenAI API https://beta.openai.com/docs/guides/moderation/overview). See Perez et al. (2022); Ganguli et al. (2022) for discussion on such risks, as well as mitigating factors and interventions. Despite the above concerns, we believe it is beneficial to publish our work on LM-written evaluations. LM-written evaluations are also valuable to good actors and efforts to deploy LMs, to catch and mitigate misuse harms as well as accidental harms. In this work alone, we believe that we surfaced several potential issues that, to our knowledge, have not been found before – related to model outputs that express power-seeking tendencies, self-preservation instincts, various strong political views, and tendencies to answer in sycophantic or less accurate ways depending on the user. Overall, our results provide evidence that LMs themselves are a valuable tool for helping us evaluate LMs.
items.zh: 我们的结果表明，恶意行为者可能能够使用LM评估LM以有害方式行事的倾向，从而加剧这种倾向。例如，恶意行为者可能评估LM倾向于说服人们接受自己的政治观点，以影响公众的观点。另一个问题是，我们的方法可能对对手有用，用于发现和利用现有模型中的弱点（例如，规避产品安全措施，如安全过滤器131313即OpenAI API的内容过滤器 https://beta.openai.com/docs/guides/moderation/overview）。参见Perez等人（2022）和Ganguli等人（2022）关于此类风险以及缓解因素和干预措施的讨论。尽管存在上述担忧，我们相信发表关于LM编写评估的工作是有益的。LM编写的评估对善意的行为者和部署LM的努力也有价值，用于捕捉和减轻滥用伤害以及意外伤害。仅在本工作中，我们认为我们发现了几个潜在问题，据我们所知，这些问题以前未被发现——与模型输出表达权力寻求倾向、自我保护本能、各种强烈的政治观点以及根据用户以谄媚或不太准确的方式回答的倾向有关。总体而言，我们的结果提供了证据，表明LM本身是帮助我们评估LM的有价值工具。

items.en: Ethan Perez developed the idea, led the project, wrote much of the paper, and conducted all experimental work not otherwise attributed. Deep Ganguli led the analysis of the data quality (§3.3) and diversity (§3.4). Karina Nguyen developed the interactive data visualization tool at evals.anthropic.com/model-written. Sam Ringer conducted the experiments on few-shot evaluation generation (§5), with help from Sandipan Kundu. Kamilė Lukošiūtė led the experiments on bias evaluation generation (§6), with help from Nicholas Schiefer and Deep Ganguli. Nicholas Schiefer helped with plotting, human evaluations, and paper writing. Samuel R. Bowman and Amanda Askell provided feedback throughout the project, and Jack Clark and Catherine Olsson gave feedback on the draft. Evan Hubinger, Deep Ganguli, Danny Hernandez, Nicholas Schiefer, Roger Grosse, Amanda Askell, and Jared Kaplan contributed ideas for evaluations in §3. Saurav Kadavath built, maintained, and debugged infrastructure used to launch data generation experiments. Edwin Chen, Scott Heiner, Craig Pettit at Surge AI recruited and managed the team of participants for crowdsourced dataset creation, dataset validation, and qualitative evaluations of model-written data. Jared Kaplan, Nicholas Schiefer, Evan Hubinger, and Deep Ganguli oversaw the project. All other listed authors contributed to the development of otherwise-unpublished models, infrastructure, or contributions that made our experiments possible.
items.zh: Ethan Perez提出了想法，领导了项目，撰写了论文的大部分内容，并进行了所有未另行归属的实验工作。Deep Ganguli领导了数据质量（§3.3）和多样性（§3.4）的分析。Karina Nguyen开发了evals.anthropic.com/model-written上的交互式数据可视化工具。Sam Ringer在Sandipan Kundu的帮助下进行了少样本评估生成实验（§5）。Kamilė Lukošiūtė在Nicholas Schiefer和Deep Ganguli的帮助下领导了偏见评估生成实验（§6）。Nicholas Schiefer帮助了绘图、人工评估和论文写作。Samuel R. Bowman和Amanda Askell在整个项目中提供了反馈，Jack Clark和Catherine Olsson对草稿提供了反馈。Evan Hubinger, Deep Ganguli, Danny Hernandez, Nicholas Schiefer, Roger Grosse, Amanda Askell和Jared Kaplan为§3中的评估贡献了想法。Saurav Kadavath构建、维护和调试了用于启动数据生成实验的基础设施。Surge AI的Edwin Chen, Scott Heiner, Craig Pettit招募并管理了众包数据集创建、数据集验证和模型编写数据定性评估的参与者团队。Jared Kaplan, Nicholas Schiefer, Evan Hubinger和Deep Ganguli监督了项目。所有其他列出的作者为其他未发表模型、基础设施或使我们的实验成为可能的贡献做出了贡献。

items.en: We thank our Surge participants for their effort in validating and creating datasets, as well as for the detailed, qualitative feedback they shared on model-written datasets. We thank Adam Jermyn, Ajeya Cotra, Alex Lyzhov, Amanda Ngo, Beth Barnes, Jacob Pfau, Julian Michael, Kath Lupante, and Miles Turpin for helpful feedback and discussion.
items.zh: 我们感谢Surge参与者在验证和创建数据集方面的努力，以及他们分享的关于模型编写数据集的详细定性反馈。我们感谢Adam Jermyn, Ajeya Cotra, Alex Lyzhov, Amanda Ngo, Beth Barnes, Jacob Pfau, Julian Michael, Kath Lupante和Miles Turpin提供的有益反馈和讨论。
